# Changelog

All notable changes to roughlogic.com are recorded here. The project follows semantic versioning.

## Unreleased

### feat: spec-v68 (Tree Care and Arborist Rigging) -- 5 tiles in calc-agriculture.js, catalog 616 -> 621; stamps 0.62.0, 2026-06-13

Equips the production tree crew Group L had only gestured at: weigh the piece, predict the shock when it is dropped, cut the hinge that steers a fell, pick the wraps that let one person lower it, and size the chip haul. A new `arboriculture` trade label is added. No new group, no new module, no new dependencies, no telemetry, no AI. All five land in `calc-agriculture.js` next to `timber-cruise`.

- **L (Forestry) +5.** `log-limb-weight` (green log / limb weight from a frustum volume and species green density: 16 in x 8 ft red oak -> 11.17 ft^3, 715 lb). `tree-rigging-shock` (dynamic load when a piece is dropped and caught: 500 lb, 3 ft drop, 30 ft rope at 5% -> 1,618 lb peak, 3.24x). `felling-notch-hinge` (open-face notch and hinge geometry: 20 in cut, 22% notch -> 4.4 in notch, 2.0 in hinge, 16 in width). `porta-wrap-friction` (capstan hold force by wrap count: 800 lb load side at 0.20 friction -> 227.7 / 64.8 / 18.5 / 5.2 lb for 1-4 wraps). `chipper-debris` (loose chip volume and haul loads: 4,400 lb green wood at 550 lb/lcy -> 8.0 lcy, 1 load).
- **Why they earn tiles.** `timber-cruise` is a stand-level board-foot estimate, not a single-piece rigging weight. The Group Z rigging tiles size crane and hardware systems; the arborist tiles size a climbing-and-lowering system. `tree-rigging-shock` computes the dynamic load no Group Z tile models (the crane tiles assume a controlled, non-shock pick), and `porta-wrap-friction` is the capstan equation for a lowering device. The single most lethal misjudgment in tree rigging -- a heavy piece dropped onto a static rope -- now has a tile that is loud about it.
- **Honesty.** The rigging-load and felling tiles carry `GOVERNANCE.worker_safety`; `log-limb-weight` and `chipper-debris` carry `GOVERNANCE.general`. ANSI Z133-2017, the FPL Wood Handbook, the arborist rigging research literature, and capstan mechanics are named, never reproduced. The shock-load tile is explicit that it UNDERESTIMATES a hard catch (a floor, not a ceiling) and that a static catch is unbounded.
- **Module / cap.** `calc-agriculture.js` cap raised 28000 -> 34000 B gz (+5 tiles, built ~32.2 KB).
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js` (new `arboriculture` trade), `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js` (5), `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+5 with cross-checks), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (27 terms), the `app.js` `AGRICULTURE_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotations, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block (every worked example, the frustum-vs-cylinder branch, the fall-factor sensitivity, the zero-elongation error seam, the wrap-count table, and every error seam), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. README catalog table + counts (Group L 19 -> 24; totals 621 tiles / 648 sitemap URLs; correctness pipeline refreshed to corpus 925 / dims 928 / fuzzer 925 / derivation 621), `docs/audit-trail.md`, and `specs/spec-v68.md` updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (26 gates), `npm test` (5,533), `npm run build` (621 tile + 25 group shells, 648-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, the worked-examples runner (+5 -> 626), `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, and render-no-nan + a11y on all five new tiles (the 4-row wrap table stacks legibly at 320 px). See [specs/spec-v68.md](specs/spec-v68.md).

### feat: spec-v67 (Earthwork and Excavation Deepening) -- 5 tiles in calc-construction.js, catalog 611 -> 616; stamps 0.61.0, 2026-06-13

Turns Group E's earthwork from a single volume number into a production bench: convert dirt among bank / loose / compacted, balance the haul fleet, pump the pit, keep the spoil off the wall, and bed the pipe. No new group, no new module, no new dependencies, no telemetry, no AI. All five land in `calc-construction.js` next to `excavation`.

- **E (Construction) +5.** `soil-swell-shrink` (bank / loose / compacted conversion: 100 bank cy at 25% swell / 15% shrink -> 125 loose, 0.80 load factor, 85 compacted). `haul-cycle-production` (truck/loader cycle and fleet match: 18.0 min cycle -> 2.78 loads/hr, 33.3 lcy/hr, 9 trucks, 300 lcy/hr fleet). `dewatering-rate` (excavation pump rate: 20x12 pit drawn 3 ft in 30 min, 40 gpm inflow, 25% margin -> 5,386 gal, 219.5 gpm, 274.4 gpm sized). `spoil-setback` (OSHA spoil setback and surcharge: 10 ft trench, 4 ft pile at 34 deg -> 5.93 ft toe spread, 10 ft setback, 15.93 ft clear). `pipe-bedding-backfill` (three-zone pipe trench take-off: 100 ft, 24 in trench, 12 in OD -> 2.47 cy / 3.46 tons bedding, 4.50 cy embedment, 22.2 cy backfill).
- **Why they earn tiles.** `excavation` returns bank cubic yards and stops; nothing converted that to trucks or placed fill, balanced a fleet, handled water, or set the spoil off the wall. `aggregate` is a flat pad volume, not the three-zone pipe-trench take-off. `pump-tdh` / `pump-sizing` give head; `dewatering-rate` gives the flow an open pit needs and defers head to `pump-tdh`.
- **Honesty.** Earthmoving tiles `GOVERNANCE.general`, naming the Caterpillar Performance Handbook soil-conversion and cycle-time methods, OSHA 1926.651(j) / Subpart P, and ASTM D2321 without reproducing them. Notes: swell / shrinkage are geotech properties; the 50-minute hour is a planning default; the inflow must come from a pumping test; 2 ft is the absolute code minimum setback; the embedment excludes the pipe's own volume.
- **Module / caps.** `calc-construction.js` cap raised 64000 -> 67000 B gz (+5 earthwork tiles, built ~65.8 KB); `citations.js` cap raised 178000 -> 182000 B gz (the v62-v67 citation entries crossed the cap).
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js` (5, `GOVERNANCE.general`), `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+5 with cross-checks), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (25 terms), the `app.js` `CONSTRUCTION_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotations, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block (every worked example, the fleet-match count, the hold-only dewatering branch, the code-floor-vs-surcharge setback, the pipe-exceeds-trench seam, and every error seam), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. README catalog table + counts (Group E 61 -> 66; totals 616 tiles / 643 sitemap URLs; correctness pipeline refreshed to corpus 920 / dims 923 / fuzzer 920 / derivation 616), `docs/audit-trail.md`, and `specs/spec-v67.md` updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (26 gates), `npm test` (5,532), `npm run build` (616 tile + 25 group shells, 643-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, the worked-examples runner (+5 -> 621), `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, and render-no-nan + a11y on all five new tiles. See [specs/spec-v67.md](specs/spec-v67.md).

### feat: spec-v66 (Group Z, Rigging and Heavy Lift -- hardware and below-the-hook) -- 6 tiles in calc-rigging.js, catalog 605 -> 611; stamps 0.60.0, 2026-06-13

Completes the **Group Z (Rigging and Heavy Lift)** founding batch at 13 tiles, adding the iron between the hook and the load to the existing `calc-rigging.js`. All carry `GOVERNANCE.rigging`. No new group, no new module, no new dependencies, no telemetry, no AI.

- **Group Z +6.** `shackle-eyebolt-wll` (hardware WLL and angular derate: shoulder eye bolt 7,000 lb at 45 deg -> 0.30, 2,100 lb, FAIL; shackle side-load and shoulder-eye-bolt derate curves). `spreader-beam` (spreader bar vs lifting beam below the hook: 10,000 lb on a 10 ft bar, top 6 ft -> 50.2 deg sling, 6,509 lb top sling, 4,167 lb bar compression, 25,000 ft-lb beam moment). `forklift-capacity-derate` (data-plate load-center derate: 5,000 lb @ 24 in handling a load at 36 in -> 3,333 lb net, 10% margin). `roller-jack-force` (push / breakaway force on rollers and skates with a grade term: 12,000 lb on skates -> 360 lb steady, 540 lb breakaway, 3 skates). `chain-lever-hoist` (hand effort and chain travel: 2,000 lb at MA 32, eff 0.85 -> 73.5 lb hand pull, 128 ft chain travel). `block-redirect-load` (resultant on a rigging block: 3,000 lb line at 90 deg -> 4,243 lb; doubled back -> 6,000 lb).
- **Why they earn tiles.** `rigging-check` (Group N) is a stage-hardware WLL pass/fail with no eye-bolt angular derate, no shackle side-load chart, and no below-the-hook member; `sling-angle` / `sling-d-d-efficiency` reduce a sling, not the hardware; `pulley-ma-gen` / `rope-ma` give mechanical advantage, not hand effort or the resultant on a block. No tile sized a spreader bar / lifting beam, derated a forklift to its load center, or found roller / jacking force.
- **Honesty.** Every tile carries `GOVERNANCE.rigging`; ASME B30.26 / B18.15 / BTH-1 / B30.20 / B56.1 / B30.16 / B30.21 are named, never reproduced. The manufacturer's rating plate and chart govern.
- **Module / caps.** `calc-rigging.js` cap raised 9000 -> 15000 B gz (Group Z complete at 13 tiles, built ~13.3 KB gz; the authorized `calc-heavylift.js` split lands if it grows further). `tools-data.js` cap raised 50000 -> 52000 B gz (the registry crossed its cap after the v62-v66 rows; lazy-loaded, not on the home view).
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js` (6, `GOVERNANCE.rigging`), `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+6 with cross-checks), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (28 terms), the `app.js` `RIGGING_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotations, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block (every worked example, the eye-bolt FAIL path, the lifting-beam moment, the forklift overload, the on-grade roller case, and every error seam), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. README catalog table + counts (Group Z 7 -> 13; totals 611 tiles / 638 sitemap URLs; correctness pipeline refreshed to corpus 915 / dims 918 / fuzzer 915 / derivation 611), `docs/audit-trail.md`, and `specs/spec-v66.md` updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (26 gates), `npm test` (5,531), `npm run build` (611 tile + 25 group shells, 638-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, the worked-examples runner (+6 -> 616), `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, and render-no-nan + a11y on all six new tiles. See [specs/spec-v66.md](specs/spec-v66.md).

### feat: spec-v65 (Group Z, Rigging and Heavy Lift -- the lift-planning core) -- new module calc-rigging.js, 7 tiles, catalog 598 -> 605; stamps 0.59.0, 2026-06-13

Opens **Group Z (Rigging and Heavy Lift)**, the first new group since the v12 allied-profession sweep, with a new `calc-rigging.js` module behind `RIGGING_RENDERERS`. Seven tiles stand up the lift-planning core. All carry `GOVERNANCE.rigging` -- the head rigger and the manufacturer's load chart govern, on every screen. No new dependencies, no telemetry, no AI.

- **New Group Z +7.** `cg-load-share` (center of gravity and per-pick-point load share: 12,000 lb at CG 40 of 120 in -> 8,000 / 4,000 lb, 33.3% imbalance, with a CG-outside flag). `crane-net-capacity` (net capacity after the OSHA 1926.1417(o) deduction stack: gross 30,000 less hook block / jib / wire rope -> 28,800 net, 22,600 hook, 78.5% -> critical-lift flag; 75 / 90 / 100% flags). `crane-ground-bearing` (outrigger / crawler ground bearing vs allowable soil bearing and the square mat side: 60,000 lb on 4 ft^2, 3,000 psf -> 15,000 psf FAIL, 4.47 ft mat). `sling-d-d-efficiency` (wire-rope sling D/d bend efficiency from the WRTB curve: 10,000 lb at D/d 3 -> 0.70, 7,000 lb). `wind-on-load` (wind force and swing angle on a suspended load via q = 0.00256 V^2: 200 ft^2 / 20 mph / 1.6 / 4,000 lb -> 1.024 psf, 327.7 lb, 4.68 deg). `tagline-force` (tag-line pull and handler count: 328 lb at 30 deg / 50 lb-person -> 378.7 lb, 8 handlers, mechanical tag). `tandem-lift-share` (two-crane load share with a per-crane derate: 40,000 lb, CG 120 of 300, 75% derate, 28,000 / 24,000 charts -> 24,000 / 16,000 share, FAIL on crane 1).
- **Why a new group.** Rigging was shallow and scattered (`crane-lift-quick`, `sling-angle`, `rigging-check`, `pulley-ma-gen`). None answered where the load's CG is and what each pick carries, the crane's net capacity after the deduction stack, whether the outriggers punch through the ground, or what the wind does to a load in the air. These seven are the planning core, none a duplicate of an existing tile.
- **Honesty.** Every tile carries `GOVERNANCE.rigging`; ASME B30.5 / B30.9 / B30.10, OSHA 1926 Subpart CC, and the WRTB Wire Rope Users Manual are named, never reproduced. The load chart is the governing document and the tiles say so.
- **New module / new group (once).** New `calc-rigging.js` (built 7,971 B gz, cap 9000) behind `RIGGING_RENDERERS`; `"Z"` appended to `app.js` GROUPS, `Z: "Rigging and Heavy Lift"` added to GROUP_NAMES and the declare block; `GROUP_SLUG.Z = "rigging-and-heavy-lift"` in build-shells; `calc-rigging.js` added to build.mjs RUNTIME_FILES and sw.js precache; the index.html browse-by-trade link; the `rigging` trade label.
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js` (new Group Z block), `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js` (7, `GOVERNANCE.rigging`), `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+7 with cross-checks), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (29 terms), the `// dims:` annotations, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block (every worked example, the CG-outside flag, the 75 / 90 / 100% flags, the ground-bearing fail-and-resize, and every error seam), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. README catalog table + counts (new Group Z row; totals 605 tiles / 25 groups / 32 modules / 632 sitemap URLs; correctness pipeline refreshed to corpus 909 / dims 912 / fuzzer 909 / derivation 605), `docs/audit-trail.md`, and `specs/spec-v65.md` updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (26 gates), `npm test` (5,530), `npm run build` (605 tile + 25 group shells, 632-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, the worked-examples runner (+7 -> 610), `check:dist` / `check:shells` (605 + 25 shells) / `check:shell-mobile`, and render-no-nan + a11y on all seven new tiles. See [specs/spec-v65.md](specs/spec-v65.md).

### feat: spec-v64 (Pipe Support Spacing and Softener Sizing) -- 2 tiles in calc-plumbing.js, catalog 596 -> 598; stamps 0.58.0, 2026-06-13

Two everyday-reference tiles that close the v61-v64 plumbing expansion, deepening Group B (Plumbing and Gas). No new group, no new module, no new dependencies, no telemetry, no AI. Both land in `calc-plumbing.js`.

- **B (Plumbing) +2.** `pipe-support-spacing` -- Hanger Spacing and Count. `max_spacing = lookup(table, material, size, orientation)` from an editable IPC 308.5 table; `hangers = ceil(run_length / max_spacing) + 1`. Worked example: 1 in type-L copper, horizontal, 24 ft run -> 6 ft max, ceil(24/6)+1 = 5 hangers; 1/2 in PEX over the same run (2.67 ft max) -> 10 hangers (twice the count -- PEX supports closer). `softener-sizing` -- Grain Load, Regeneration Interval, and Salt. `comp_hardness = hardness + iron x 4` (WQA); `grain_load = people x use x comp_hardness`; `days_between = floor(capacity / grain_load)`; `annual_salt = salt_per_regen x 365 / days_between`. Worked example: 4 people at 75 gal/day, 20 gpg, 2 ppm iron, 32,000-grain capacity, 15 lb salt -> 28 gpg, 8,400 grains/day, 3 days, 1,825 lb/yr; soft-ish 12 gpg / no iron -> 3,600 grains/day, 8 days. A non-positive run/spacing, occupancy, use, or capacity, or a negative hardness/iron errors.
- **Why they earn tiles.** `pipe-spacing-rack` sets the lateral clearance between parallel insulated lines on a rack -- not the longitudinal support interval along one run, which is `pipe-support-spacing` (IPC 308.5). No tile sizes a water softener; the water-treatment tiles (`langelier-index`, `dilution`, pool chemistry) address scaling and dosing, not ion-exchange grain capacity or regeneration interval.
- **Honesty.** Both `GOVERNANCE.general`, naming IPC 2021 Table 308.5 / MSS SP-58 and NSF/ANSI 44 / WQA without reproducing them. Notes: the spacing values are maximums (closer is always allowed and required near valves and direction changes); vertical piping is also supported at each story; iron compensation is ~4 gpg per ppm and the capacity must match the programmed salt dose.
- **Module / cap.** `calc-plumbing.js` cap raised 57000 -> 60000 B gz (lazy-loaded; the two rows took the built copy to ~57.7 KB gz).
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+2 pinned cases), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (12 terms), the `app.js` `PLUMBING_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotations, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block (both worked examples, the PEX and soft-water cross-checks, and every error seam), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. README catalog table + counts (Group B 48 -> 50; totals 598 tiles / 624 sitemap URLs; correctness pipeline refreshed to corpus 902 / dims 905 / fuzzer 902 / derivation 598), `docs/audit-trail.md`, and `specs/spec-v64.md` updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (26 gates; corpus 902 / dims 905/905 / fuzzer 902/902 / derivation 598/598 / citation-coverage 598; check-readme-counts agrees 598 / 624), `npm test`, `npm run build` (598 tile + 24 group shells, 624-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, the worked-examples runner (+2 -> 603), `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile` (624 shells, zero horizontal scroll at 320 px), and render-no-nan + a11y on both new tiles (read to the value: 6 ft / 5 hangers; 28 gpg / 8,400 grains/day / 3 days / 1,825 lb/yr). See [specs/spec-v64.md](specs/spec-v64.md).

### feat: spec-v63 (Gas Appliance Demand and Relief Discharge) -- 2 tiles in calc-plumbing.js, catalog 594 -> 596; stamps 0.57.0, 2026-06-13

Two safety-and-demand tiles bracketing fuel-gas and water-heater work, deepening Group B (Plumbing and Gas). No new group, no new module, no new dependencies, no telemetry, no AI. Both land in `calc-plumbing.js`.

- **B (Plumbing) +2.** `gas-appliance-demand` -- Connected Load to CFH. `total_btuh = sum(appliance input ratings)`; `cfh = total_btuh / heating_value`. Worked example: furnace 100,000 + water heater 40,000 + range 65,000 + dryer 35,000 = 240,000 BTU/hr -> 240 CFH on natural gas (1,000 BTU/ft^3); the same load on propane (2,516 BTU/ft^3) -> 95.4 CFH (fewer CFH, a smaller pipe for the same length). An empty list, a negative input, or a non-positive heating value errors. `tpr-discharge` -- Water-Heater T&P Relief and Discharge Check. `rating_ok = valve_rating >= heater_input`; the discharge line is the full valve outlet, never reduced. Worked example: heater input 50,000 BTU/hr, T&P valve marked 150,000, 3/4 in outlet -> PASS, 3/4 in discharge, 100,000 BTU/hr margin, plus the IPC 504.6 checklist; a salvaged 40,000 BTU/hr valve on the same heater -> FAIL (undersized). A non-positive heater input or valve rating errors.
- **Why they earn tiles.** `gas-pipe-sizing` sizes a branch from a CFH and a length but takes the CFH as given; `gas-appliance-demand` builds it from the appliance schedule (the upstream step). `wh-expansion-tank` and `water-heater-recovery` size the expansion tank and recovery rate; neither checks the T&P relief valve or its discharge line, which is `tpr-discharge`'s sole job and the top water-heater inspection failure.
- **Honesty.** Both `GOVERNANCE.general`, naming IFGC 2021 Section 402 / NFPA 54 and IPC 2021 Section 504 / ANSI Z21.22 without reproducing them. Notes: fuel-gas piping is sized for the full connected load unless the AHJ accepts a demand factor (IFGC 402.2); the discharge pipe is the full valve outlet, never reduced, and serves no other valve (IPC 504.6).
- **Module / cap.** `calc-plumbing.js` cap raised 54000 -> 57000 B gz (lazy-loaded; the two rows took the built copy to ~55.0 KB gz).
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+2 pinned cases), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (12 terms), the `app.js` `PLUMBING_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotations (the appliance list field uses `makeTextarea` per the multiline-input gate), a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block (both worked examples, the propane and undersized-valve cross-checks, and every error seam), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. README catalog table + counts (Group B 46 -> 48; totals 596 tiles / 622 sitemap URLs; correctness pipeline refreshed to corpus 900 / dims 903 / fuzzer 900 / derivation 596), `docs/audit-trail.md`, and `specs/spec-v63.md` updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (26 gates; corpus 900 / dims 903/903 / fuzzer 900/900 / derivation 596/596 / citation-coverage 596; check-readme-counts agrees 596 / 622), `npm test`, `npm run build` (596 tile + 24 group shells, 622-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, the worked-examples runner (+2 -> 601), `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile` (622 shells, zero horizontal scroll at 320 px), and render-no-nan + a11y on both new tiles (read to the value: 240 CFH; PASS, 3/4 in discharge). See [specs/spec-v63.md](specs/spec-v63.md).

### feat: spec-v62 (Roof Drainage and Sump/Ejector Sizing) -- 2 tiles in calc-plumbing.js, catalog 592 -> 594; stamps 0.56.0, 2026-06-13

Two storm-and-below-grade take-offs, deepening Group B (Plumbing and Gas). No new group, no new module, no new dependencies, no telemetry, no AI. Both land in `calc-plumbing.js`.

- **B (Plumbing) +2.** `roof-drain-sizing` -- Roof Area to Storm GPM and Leader Size. `gpm = roof_area x rainfall_rate x 0.0104` (GPM per ft^2 per in/hr); the vertical leader and the sloped horizontal storm drain are then sized to the smallest pipe whose capacity meets the flow, against editable capacity tables. Worked example: 5,000 ft^2 roof, 4 in/hr design rainfall -> 208 GPM, 6 in vertical leader, 8 in horizontal storm drain at 1/4 in/ft; a Miami-class 6 in/hr cross-check -> 312 GPM, pushing both sizes up one increment (8 in / 10 in). A non-positive area or rainfall, a non-monotonic capacity table, or a non-finite input errors. `sump-basin-sizing` -- Basin Drawdown and Pump-Cycle Check. `area = (PI/4)(dia/12)^2`; `drawdown_gal = area x (band/12) x 7.48`; `run = drawdown / (pump - inflow) x 60`; `fill = drawdown / inflow x 60`; `cycles_per_hr = 3600 / (run + fill)`. Worked example: 24 in basin, 12 in float spread, 10 GPM inflow, 30 GPM pump, 60 s min run -> 23.5 gal drawdown, 70.5 s run, 141 s fill, 17.0 cycles/hr, adequate; raising inflow to 25 GPM gives a 282 s run, 56 s fill (slower-cycling, still adequate). Any non-positive dimension, a non-positive pump, **inflow >= pump** (the pump never empties the basin), or a non-finite input errors.
- **Why they earn tiles.** `stormwater-rational` returns site peak runoff (cfs) from C x i x A -- a civil/grading number, not the building plumbing path from roof area to leader and storm-drain size per IPC 1106. `pump-sizing` / `pump-tdh` solve head and flow for a pump curve but never compute a basin's drawdown volume or its start/stop cycle against an inflow.
- **Honesty.** Both `GOVERNANCE.general`, naming IPC 2021 Section 1106 (Tables 1106.2 / 1106.3 / 1106.6) and Section 712, and the Hydraulic Institute cycling guidance, without reproducing them. Notes: rainfall is the locale-specific 100-yr / 1-hr value from IPC Figure 1106.1 (not a national default); the capacity tables are editable conservative approximations to tune to the published edition; overflow drains / scuppers (IPC 1107) are a separate required path; a sewage ejector's 2 in solids passage and vent (IPC 712.3-712.4) are out of scope.
- **Module / cap.** `calc-plumbing.js` cap raised 51000 -> 54000 B gz (lazy-loaded; the two rows took the built copy to ~52.4 KB gz).
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+2 pinned cases), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (12 terms), the `app.js` `PLUMBING_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotations, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block (both worked examples, the 6 in/hr and 25 GPM cross-checks, and every error seam), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. README catalog table + counts (Group B 44 -> 46; totals 594 tiles / 620 sitemap URLs; correctness pipeline refreshed to corpus 898 / dims 901 / fuzzer 898 / derivation 594), `docs/audit-trail.md`, and `specs/spec-v62.md` updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (26 gates; corpus 898 / dims 901/901 / fuzzer 898/898 / derivation 594/594 / citation-coverage 594; check-readme-counts agrees 594 / 620), `npm test`, `npm run build` (594 tile + 24 group shells, 620-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, the worked-examples runner (+2 -> 599), `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile` (620 shells, zero horizontal scroll at 320 px), and render-no-nan + a11y on both new tiles (read to the value: 208 GPM / 6 in / 8 in; 23.5 gal / 70.5 s / 17.0 cycles/hr, adequate). See [specs/spec-v62.md](specs/spec-v62.md).

### feat: spec-v61 (Water-Supply Demand and Pressure Budget) -- 2 tiles in calc-plumbing.js, catalog 590 -> 592; stamps 0.55.0, 2026-06-13

Two tiles at the head of the domestic water-supply design chain, deepening Group B (Plumbing and Gas). No new group, no new module, no new dependencies, no telemetry, no AI. Both land in `calc-plumbing.js`.

- **B (Plumbing) +2.** `wsfu-demand` -- Probable Peak Demand from Fixture Units. Hunter's-curve interpolation `gpm = interpolate(curve, wsfu)` with separate flush-tank and flush-valve curves (a flushometer system peaks higher for the same WSFU). Worked example: 120 WSFU flush-valve between (100, 55) and (150, 66) -> 59.4 GPM; flush-tank between (100, 43) and (150, 51) -> 46.2 GPM. The curve ships as editable breakpoints (an approximation of IPC 2021 Appendix E Table E103.3(2) / NBS BMS65), not a transcribed table; a non-monotonic curve, a negative WSFU, or a non-finite input errors. `supply-pressure-budget` -- Available Pressure at the Critical Fixture. `elevation_loss = fixture_height x 0.433` (psi/ft water column); `available = street - elevation - meter - bfp - friction`; `headroom = available - fixture_min`; `adequate = headroom >= 0`. Worked example: street 60 psi, 30 ft up, meter 8, friction 12, min 8 -> 12.99 psi elevation loss, 27.01 psi available, 19.01 psi headroom, adequate; a flushometer's 25 psi minimum leaves 2.01 psi (adequate, no margin).
- **Why they earn tiles.** `pipe-sizing` rolls fixture units into a diameter but exposes no standalone WSFU -> GPM probable-demand figure (the number every downstream sizing decision starts from); `static-pressure-piping` reports one run's static loss and `friction-loss` one segment, but neither composes street + elevation + meter + backflow + developed-length into a residual against a required minimum with a go / no-go.
- **Honesty.** Both `GOVERNANCE.general`, naming Hunter's curve / NBS BMS65, IPC 2021 Appendix E and Section 604, and the ASPE PEDH Vol. 2 without reproducing them. Notes: WSFU come from the fixture schedule and the bundled curve is an editable approximation; use the *minimum* recorded street pressure, flush-valve / tankless fixtures need 15-25 psi, and IPC 604 caps static pressure at 80 psi (a PRV adds its own loss).
- **Module / cap.** `calc-plumbing.js` cap raised 49000 -> 51000 B gz (lazy-loaded; the two rows took it to 49378 B).
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+2 pinned cases), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (9 terms; reused-term collisions with `pipe-sizing` avoided), the `app.js` `PLUMBING_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotations, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block (both worked examples, the flush-tank cross-check, the non-monotonic-curve and pressure error seams), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. README catalog table + counts (Group B 42 -> 44; totals 592 tiles / 618 sitemap URLs; correctness pipeline refreshed to corpus 896 / dims 899 / fuzzer 896 / derivation 592), `docs/audit-trail.md`, and `specs/spec-v61.md` updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (26 gates; wiring 31 modules / 592 entries; corpus 896 / dims 899/899 / fuzzer 896/896 / derivation 592/592 / citation-coverage 592; check-readme-counts agrees 592 / 618), `npm test`, `npm run build` (592 tile + 24 group shells, 618-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, the worked-examples runner (+2 -> 597), `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile` (618 shells, zero horizontal scroll at 320 px), and render-no-nan + a11y on both new tiles (read to the value: 59.4 GPM; 13.0 / 27.0 / 19.0 psi, adequate). See [specs/spec-v61.md](specs/spec-v61.md).

### feat: spec-v60 (Water-Loss Documentation) -- 2 tiles in calc-restoration.js, catalog 588 -> 590; stamps 0.54.0, 2026-06-13

Two documentation tiles completing the v58-v60 Group D batch. No new group, no new module, no new dependencies, no telemetry, no AI. Both land in `calc-restoration.js`.

- **D (Restoration) +2.** `moisture-dry-goal` -- Dry Standard vs Affected Reading. The IICRC S500 dry standard is a comparison to unaffected like-material: `delta = affected - reference`; `at_dry_standard = delta <= acceptable_delta`; `points_to_go = max(0, delta - acceptable_delta)`. Worked example: reference 12, affected 35, delta-allow 4 -> 23 above standard, continue drying, 19 to go; affected 15 -> delta 3, at dry standard, 0 to go; wood 11% vs 14% at delta 2 -> 1 point to go. `flood-cut-quantity` -- Flood-Cut Demolition Take-Off. `drywall_ft2 = run x (cut_height/12) x faces`; `sheets_4x8 = ceil(drywall_ft2 / 32)`; `baseboard_lf = run`; `insulation_ft2 = insulated ? run x (cut_height/12) : 0`. Worked example: 60 LF, 24 in, one side, insulated -> 120 ft2 drywall, 4 sheets, 60 LF base, 120 ft2 batt; two-sided -> 240 ft2, 8 sheets.
- **Why they earn tiles.** Group D tracked the drying *process* (`drying-log`, `grains-removed`) but had no tile for the two documentation numbers an estimator writes on the moisture map: the per-material dry goal and the demolition quantity. `drying-goal` targets indoor *air* GPP (a psychrometric set point), not the *material* moisture-content dry standard; no tile computed flood-cut quantities.
- **Honesty.** Both `GOVERNANCE.general`, naming IICRC S500-2021 without reproducing it. The dry-standard note requires the same material / meter / mode / scale for the reference and affected readings. The flood-cut note states the cut height is a field decision driven by the highest moisture reading (not a fixed 2 ft rule), Category 3 may require removing all wet porous material, and pre-1980 structures need a lead / asbestos assessment first.
- **Module / cap.** `calc-restoration.js` cap raised 26500 -> 27000 B gz (lazy-loaded; the batch took it to 26092 B). This completes the v58-v60 Group D batch (six rows; 19858 -> 26092 B gz across the three specs, the authorized `calc-mold.js` split deferred as the module remains under cap with headroom).
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+2 pinned cases), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (8 terms), the `app.js` `RESTORATION_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotations, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block (both worked examples, the dry / wood / two-sided cross-checks, every error seam), the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index, and the `citations.test.js` Group D count (23 -> 25). README catalog table + counts (Group D 23 -> 25; totals 590 tiles / 616 sitemap URLs; correctness pipeline refreshed to corpus 894 / dims 897 / fuzzer 894 / derivation 590), `docs/audit-trail.md`, and `specs/spec-v60.md` updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (26 gates; wiring 31 modules / 590 entries; corpus 894 / dims 897/897 / fuzzer 894/894 / derivation 590/590 / citation-coverage 590; check-readme-counts agrees 590 / 616), `npm test`, `npm run build` (590 tile + 24 group shells, 616-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, the worked-examples runner (+2 -> 595), `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile` (616 shells, zero horizontal scroll at 320 px), and render-no-nan + a11y on both new tiles (read to the value: 23 / continue drying / 19; 120 ft2 / 4 sheets / 60 LF / 120 ft2). See [specs/spec-v60.md](specs/spec-v60.md).

### feat: spec-v59 (Remediation Chemistry and Air Sampling) -- 2 tiles in calc-restoration.js, catalog 586 -> 588; stamps 0.53.0, 2026-06-13

Two field tiles deepening Group D (Water Damage and Mold Restoration) around the cleaning step. No new group, no new module, no new dependencies, no telemetry, no AI. Both land in `calc-restoration.js`.

- **D (Restoration) +2.** `antimicrobial-dilution` -- Antimicrobial Mix and Coverage. From an area, the label coverage rate, the label dilution, and the sprayer tank size: `finished_gal = area / coverage`; `conc_oz_per_gal = oz_per_gal` (mode A) or `128/(N+1)` (mode B, 1:N by volume, 128 fl oz/gal); `concentrate_oz = finished_gal x conc_oz_per_gal`; `water_gal = finished_gal - concentrate_oz/128`; `tanks = ceil(finished_gal / tank)`; per-tank dose = `conc_oz_per_gal x tank`. Worked example: 400 ft2 at 200 ft2/gal, 4 oz/gal, 1.5 gal tank -> 2.0 gal finished, 8.0 oz concentrate, 1.94 gal water, 2 fills, 6.0 oz per tank; mode B 1:64 -> 1.97 oz/gal. `air-sample-volume` -- Air Sample Run Time and Volume. `run_time_min = target_volume / flow`; total volume = target x count; total pump time = run_time x count (sequential). Worked example: 15 L/min, 75 L, 3 cassettes -> 5.0 min (300 s) each, 225 L total, 15.0 min total; Andersen N6 (28.3 L/min, 28.3 L) -> 1.0 min.
- **Governance seams.** `antimicrobial-dilution` uses `GOVERNANCE.pesticide` -- read and follow the EPA-registered product label (the label is the law under FIFRA); the coverage and dilution defaults are placeholders, not a recommendation; antimicrobials do not substitute for the physical removal of growth (IICRC S520). `air-sample-volume` uses `GOVERNANCE.general`, naming ASTM D7391 (spore-trap method), the cassette manufacturer's instructions, and AIHA-accredited laboratory analysis; the calibrated rotameter flow governs.
- **Module / cap.** `calc-restoration.js` cap raised 24000 -> 26500 B gz (lazy-loaded; the two rows took it to 24339 B). Group letter independent of module.
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+2 pinned cases), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (10 terms), the `app.js` `RESTORATION_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotations, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block (both worked examples, the mode-B ratio, the N6 cross-check, every error seam), the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index, and the `citations.test.js` Group D count (21 -> 23). README catalog table + counts (Group D 21 -> 23; totals 588 tiles / 614 sitemap URLs; correctness pipeline refreshed to corpus 892 / dims 895 / fuzzer 892 / derivation 588), `docs/audit-trail.md`, and `specs/spec-v59.md` updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (26 gates; wiring 31 modules / 588 entries; corpus 892 / dims 895/895 / fuzzer 892/892 / derivation 588/588 / citation-coverage 588; check-readme-counts agrees 588 / 614), `npm test`, `npm run build` (588 tile + 24 group shells, 614-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, the worked-examples runner (+2 -> 593), `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile` (614 shells, zero horizontal scroll at 320 px), and render-no-nan + a11y on both new tiles (read to the value: 2.0 gal / 8.0 oz / 2 fills; 5.0 min / 225 L / 15.0 min). See [specs/spec-v59.md](specs/spec-v59.md).

### feat: spec-v58 (Mold Remediation Scoping) -- 2 tiles in calc-restoration.js, catalog 584 -> 586; stamps 0.52.0, 2026-06-13

Two first-principles tiles deepening Group D (Water Damage and Mold Restoration) at the front of a mold job. No new group, no new module, no new dependencies, no telemetry, no AI. Both land in `calc-restoration.js` beside `mold` and `water-classes`.

- **D (Restoration) +2.** `mold-remediation-level` -- Remediation Scope by Affected Area. A deterministic lookup from affected square footage plus three flags (porous material, HVAC involvement, vulnerable occupant) to the EPA 402-K-01-001 band (small < 10 ft2 / medium 10-100 / large > 100), the NYC DOHMH level (I-IV, with any HVAC involvement overriding to Level V), and the derived controls: containment (limited / full), PPE tier (N95 -> half-face P100 + suit -> full-face / PAPR), whether an independent environmental professional (IEP) assessment is advised (area > 100, HVAC, or vulnerable occupant), and whether post-remediation verification (clearance) is advised (medium/large, HVAC, or vulnerable occupant). Worked example: 45 ft2 of moldy drywall, no HVAC, healthy occupant -> medium band, Level III, full containment, half-face P100, optional IEP, clearance recommended. Cross-checks: 6 ft2 hard surface -> small / Level I / limited / N95 / optional IEP / optional clearance; 250 ft2 -> large / Level IV / full-face PAPR / IEP + clearance recommended; any HVAC job -> Level V with IEP + clearance regardless of area. Non-positive or non-finite area returns an error; the five-band lookup cannot build an unbounded structure. `mold-conditions` -- IICRC S520 Condition Reference. A reference page parallel to `water-classes`: the three S520 Conditions (1 normal fungal ecology, the goal state; 2 settled spores dispersed from a Condition 3 area; 3 actual growth, active or dormant, visible or hidden) in original plain English, with remediation defined as returning Condition 2 and 3 areas to Condition 1.
- **Why they earn tiles.** Group D sized the drying and containment equipment and reported growth thresholds (`mold`), but had no tile mapping affected area to a remediation scope (the first decision an estimator makes) and none stating the S520 Condition framework (the language a remediation protocol is written in). A concept-check for remediation-level / scope-of-work / S520-condition / square-foot-bands returned nothing; `mold` is growth-threshold risk, `water-classes` is the S500 water vocabulary, and `ppe` maps water *category* to PPE (this maps mold *area* to a control set).
- **Honesty seams.** Both use `GOVERNANCE.general` and name EPA 402-K-01-001 (free at epa.gov/mold), the NYC DOHMH guidelines, and IICRC S520-2024 (licensed) without reproducing them. The level output is scope guidance, not an assessment: the note states areas are summed visible + reasonably suspected growth, the highest moisture reading and the protocol govern the cut line, hidden Condition 3 growth can exceed the visible estimate, and a vulnerable occupant raises controls independent of area.
- **Module / cap.** `calc-restoration.js` crossed its 22000 B gz cap on the two rows (22006 B); cap raised 22000 -> 24000 (lazy-loaded module, not in the home payload). Group letter independent of module per the v28/v36/v39 precedent.
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (the pinned 45 ft2 case for `mold-remediation-level`; a reference smoke row for `mold-conditions`), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (11 terms), the `app.js` `RESTORATION_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotations, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block (the EPA band / NYC level / control cross-checks, the HVAC Level V override, the error seam, plus the `mold-conditions` reference smoke), the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index, and the `citations.test.js` Group D count (19 -> 21). README catalog table + counts (Group D 19 -> 21; totals 586 tiles / 612 sitemap URLs; the correctness-pipeline Mermaid + table refreshed to live corpus 890 / dims 893 / fuzzer 890 / derivation 586), `docs/audit-trail.md`, and `specs/spec-v58.md` updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (26 gates; wiring 31 modules / 586 entries; corpus 890 / dims 893/893 / fuzzer 890/890 / derivation 586/586 / citation-coverage 586; check-readme-counts agrees 586 / 612), `npm test` (unit suite), `npm run build` (586 tile + 24 group shells, 612-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, the worked-examples runner (+2 rows -> 591), `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile` (612 shells, zero horizontal scroll at 320 px), and the render-no-nan + a11y Chromium checks on both new tiles (45 ft2 -> medium / Level III / full containment / half-face P100 / clearance recommended, read to the value). See [specs/spec-v58.md](specs/spec-v58.md).

### docs: README data-integrity diagram (two SHA-256 hash chains, two trust boundaries) -- no code/data/output change; stamps 0.51.2, 2026-06-13

Documentation-only visualization pass. No tile, code, data, or shipped-output change (catalog stays 584). Verified the whole green bar still holds before and after (full `npm run lint` 26 gates, `npm test` 5,521 unit tests, `npm run build`, `npm run data:verify`, and the runtime sweeps: render-no-nan 584 tiles, responsive-stress 68-case Chromium/WebKit 320px, a11y 585-case -- all green; the catalog and runtime layer are clean, no bug to fix).

- **The integrity check was described in prose but never diagrammed, and the one diagram that touched it conflated two distinct chains.** The component diagram drew "hashed at build" as a single `integrity.json -> App` edge, hiding that `scripts/build-data.mjs` emits **two** independent SHA-256 chains for two unrelated failure modes. Added a `flowchart` to the System-design section ("Data integrity: two hash chains, two trust boundaries") that draws the real shape and a short rationale paragraph.
- **What the diagram pins (verified against source).** Build time: one `build-data.mjs` pass writes per-folder `manifest.json`, `scripts/expected-hashes.json` (**123** shard + manifest hashes), and the `data/integrity.json` sidecar (**18** per-folder manifest hashes). Boundary 1 (CI, fails closed): `npm run data:verify` (`scripts/verify-integrity.mjs`) rehashes all 123 shards and exits non-zero on any mismatch, so unreviewed data never reaches `main`. Boundary 2 (runtime, fails open): `integrity.js` recomputes the 18 manifest hashes over SubtleCrypto on startup and shows a non-blocking banner on drift, but skips the check (rather than blocking the calculators) in an insecure context with no SubtleCrypto.
- **Not touched.** No catalog, count, or gate-anchored number changes; the README sits outside the home payload, so byte impact is zero. `grep-checks` (em-dash ban) and `check-readme-counts` pass unchanged.

### feat: spec-v57 (Equal Spacing Layout) -- 1 tile in calc-layout.js, catalog 583 -> 584; stamps 0.51.0, 2026-06-13

A single first-principles, hand-verifiable tile deepening Group G (Cross-Trade Utilities) with one of the most-performed layout calculations on a job site. No new group, no new module, no new dependencies. Lands in `calc-layout.js` (the layout & shop-geometry bench), next to `decimal-to-fraction` and the polygon / bolt-circle / arc family.

- **G (Cross-Trade) +1.** `equal-spacing` -- Equal Spacing Layout. Space balusters, pickets, studs, shelf pins, or layout marks evenly in a run: N items of width w have N+1 equal gaps of `(run - N x w)/(N+1)` and a center-to-center pitch of `gap + w`. Two modes: **count from a maximum gap** (the railing case: the smallest N whose gap stays at or below a limit, `N = ceil((run - gmax)/(w + gmax))`) and **gap from a desired count** (the divide-a-run-into-equal-parts case). Item width 0 lays out division points. Reports the count, exact gap, pitch, and item-center positions. Worked example: a 60 in run, 1.5 in balusters, 4 in max gap (IRC R312.1.3 4-inch sphere) -> 11 balusters, 3.625 in gap, 5.125 in pitch (10 would give 4.09 in, over the limit). Cross-checks: dividing a 100 in run into 6 equal parts -> 16.667 in gap, middle mark at 50 in; an over-fitting count returns a finite negative gap flagged "does not fit." Run <= 0, width < 0, max gap <= 0, count < 1, and non-finite inputs return an error; the position list is bounded to 200 entries.
- **Why it earns a tile.** Every deck railing, fence, wainscot, shelf-pin row, and stud wall needs this arithmetic, and the catalog had nothing for it -- `decimal-to-fraction` converts one measurement, `deck-ledger-fasteners` is a code-table spacing for one connector, and no tile divides a run into equal parts. The concept-check returned no spacing / baluster / picket / divide-into-parts tile.
- **Module / cap.** Lands in `calc-layout.js` (91.5% of its 13500 B cap after the row; no bump, joins the watch list). Group letter independent of module per the v28/v36/v39 precedent.
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json`, `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (5), the `app.js` `LAYOUT_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotation, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block (baluster example, count-mode division, over-fit flag, error seams), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. README catalog table + counts (Group G 50 -> 51; totals 584 tiles / 610 sitemap URLs; plus a fix to two half-stale Mermaid derivation-index nodes), `docs/audit-trail.md`, and `specs/spec-v57.md` updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (26 gates; wiring 31 modules / 584 entries; corpus 888 / dims 891 / derivation 584/584 / citation-coverage 584; check-readme-counts agrees 584 / 610), `npm test` (**5,521 unit tests**, +1), `npm run build` (584 tile + 24 group shells, 610-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile` (610 shells, zero horizontal scroll at 320 px), and the render-no-nan + a11y Chromium checks on the new tile all green.

### chore: spec-v56 (calc-fab.js Module Split) -- platform-only, catalog holds at 583; stamps 0.50.1, 2026-06-13

Platform-only housekeeping in the spirit of spec-v36 / spec-v39 / spec-v42: **adds no tiles, removes none, changes no calculator output** (catalog stays 583). Only the on-disk module layout changes. No new dependencies, no telemetry, no AI.

- **Why.** `calc-fab.js` had reached **96.3% of its 20,000 B gzip cap** after the layout family grew across v32/v37/v38/v44/v47/v55 (bolt-circle, sine-bar, thread-pitch, circular-arc, circle-from-3-points, polygon-miter) on top of the v26 pipefitter's bench, v27 center-of-gravity, v33 decimal-to-fraction, and the v39 conduit suite. The next layout tile would have breached the cap, so the documented per-tile-split remediation came due (continued cap-bumping is the anti-pattern those splits exist to end).
- **The split.** The eight pure layout / shop-geometry tiles (`center-of-gravity-2point`, `bolt-circle`, `decimal-to-fraction`, `sine-bar`, `thread-pitch`, `circular-arc`, `circle-from-3-points`, `polygon-miter`) relocated into a new thematic module **`calc-layout.js`** (Layout & shop-geometry bench) behind `LAYOUT_RENDERERS`. `calc-fab.js` keeps the seven pipe & conduit fabrication tiles (`pipe-fitting-takeout`, `pipe-miter-cut`, `pipe-template-wrap`, `flange-bolt-torque`, `conduit-offset`, `conduit-saddle`, `conduit-90-stub`). **All tiles keep their group letter** (G for the pipe/layout tiles, A for conduit; group is independent of module, the v28/v36/v39 precedent); ids, citations, worked examples, dimensional annotations, and behavior are byte-for-byte unchanged. The cleavage is self-contained: only `_finiteGuard` is shared (copied into the new module); `_bcGcd` (decimal-to-fraction) and `_V38_COS30` (thread-pitch) move with their tiles, the v26 flange helpers stay.
- **Sizes / cap.** After the split `calc-fab.js` is **10.1 KB gz** (cap lowered 20000 -> 13000 B, 77.7%) and `calc-layout.js` is **10.6 KB gz** (new cap 13500 B, 78.6%); both lazy-loaded, home-view payload unaffected. Module count **30 -> 31**.
- **Repointed + gated.** `app.js` declare (eight tiles moved to a new `LAYOUT_RENDERERS` declare), `scripts/build.mjs` `FILES`, `sw.js` `SHELL_ASSETS`, `scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs` caps, `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js` (eight imports), `test/unit/calc-v27.test.js`, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. README module-count references (30 -> 31, file-tree, architecture diagram) and `specs/spec-v56.md` + `docs/audit-trail.md` updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (26 gates; wiring **31 renderer modules / 583 tile-id entries**; check-readme-counts agrees 583 / 31 / 609; check-module-sizes passes both new caps; check:dist resolves the new module; check:sw-precache confirms it precached), `npm test` (**5,520 unit tests**, unchanged), `npm run build` (583 tile + 24 group shells, 609 URLs, 797 dist files), `npm run data:verify`, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile` (609 shells, no 320px horizontal scroll), and a render-no-nan + a11y Chromium pass over the eight moved tiles + a kept pipe/conduit tile (identical output through the new routing). See [specs/spec-v56.md](specs/spec-v56.md).

### feat: spec-v55 (Regular Polygon Miter and Layout) -- 1 tile in calc-fab.js, catalog 582 -> 583; stamps 0.50.0, 2026-06-13

A single first-principles, hand-verifiable tile deepening Group G (Cross-Trade Utilities) with the regular-polygon frame layout the catalog only half-covered. No new group, no new module, no new dependencies. Lands in `calc-fab.js` (the Fabrication & Layout bench), next to `bolt-circle`, `circular-arc`, and `circle-from-3-points`.

- **G (Cross-Trade) +1.** `polygon-miter` -- Regular Polygon Miter and Layout. Build any N-sided frame (octagon column wrap, hexagon planter, picture frame, segmented turning ring, gazebo facet): each of the N pieces is mitered at `180/N` degrees off square at both ends, the interior corner is `(N-2) x 180/N`, and the side relates to the across-flats width by `s = flats x tan(180/N)` and the across-corners diameter by `s = corners x sin(180/N)`, with perimeter and area. The user gives the side count and a target size as one of three labeled dimensions (side / across-flats / across-corners). Worked example: a hexagon with side 12 in -> 30 deg miter, 120 deg interior, 24 in across corners, 20.7846 in across flats, 374.123 in^2. Reproduces the known shop miters (square 45, hexagon 30, octagon 22.5). Fewer than 3 sides, more than 360, a non-positive size, or non-finite inputs return an error.
- **Why it earns a tile.** The `geometry` tile already does regular-polygon area (hexagon) and a polygon perimeter from a side list, and `compound-miter` (v54) does sprung crown, but nothing turned a side count into a miter saw setting or sized a regular polygon to a target across-flats / across-corners dimension. The geometry tile doing half the job is the evidence the gap is real.
- **Module / cap.** Lands in `calc-fab.js` (96.3% of its 20000 B cap; no bump -- now the next split candidate on the watch list). Group letter is independent of module per the v28/v36/v39 precedent.
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json`, `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (5), the `app.js` `FAB_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotation, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block pinning the hexagon, the octagon and across-corners size round-trips, and the error seams, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. README catalog table + counts (Group G 49 -> 50; totals 583 tiles / 609 sitemap URLs), `docs/audit-trail.md`, and `specs/spec-v55.md` updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (26 gates; wiring 30 modules / 583 entries; corpus 887 / dims 890 / derivation 583/583 / citation-coverage 583; check-readme-counts agrees 583 / 609), `npm test` (**5,520 unit tests**, +1), `npm run build` (583 tile + 24 group shells, 609-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile` (609 shells, zero horizontal scroll at 320 px), and the render-no-nan + a11y Chromium checks on the new tile all green.

### feat: spec-v54 (Compound Miter for Crown Molding) -- 1 tile in calc-shop.js, catalog 581 -> 582; stamps 0.49.0, 2026-06-13

A single first-principles, hand-verifiable tile deepening Group E (Carpentry and Construction) with the one finish-carpentry calculation the catalog had no substitute for: the two saw settings to cut crown molding flat on the table. No new group, no new module, no new dependencies. Lands in `calc-shop.js` (which already hosts Group E welding/sheet-metal tiles and has the headroom `calc-construction.js`, at 94% of cap, does not).

- **E (Carpentry) +1.** `compound-miter` -- Compound Miter (Crown Molding). Crown installed sprung against the wall needs a compound cut, a miter (table swing) AND a bevel (blade tilt): `miter = atan(tan(corner/2) x sin(spring))` and `bevel = asin(cos(spring) x cos(corner/2))` from the molding spring angle (38 or 45 degrees) and the wall corner angle (90 for a square corner). Worked example: 38-degree spring at a 90-degree corner -> 31.62-degree miter, 33.86-degree bevel; cross-checked at 45/90 -> 35.26 / 30.00. Both reproduce the standard published compound-miter chart to the digit, and the tile handles the out-of-square corners a printed two-row chart cannot. The angle magnitudes are the same for inside and outside corners; only the workpiece orientation changes. Degenerate inputs (spring outside 0-90, corner outside 0-180, non-finite) return an error.
- **Why it earns a tile.** Finish carpenters reach for a printed compound-miter chart for exactly two spring profiles at exactly one (90-degree) corner and have nothing for real-house corners that are not square. The catalog has `pipe-miter-cut` (lobster-back pipe), `roof-pitch`, and `rafter`, but none turn a spring angle into saw settings. That printed chart is both the evidence the gap is real and the independent worked example the tile is verified against.
- **Module / cap.** Lands in `calc-shop.js` (90.0% of its 16000 B cap; no bump). Group letter is independent of module per the v28/v36/v39 precedent.
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json`, `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (5), the `app.js` `SHOP_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotation, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block pinning both chart profiles and the error seams, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. README catalog table + counts (Group E 60 -> 61; totals 582 tiles / 608 sitemap URLs), `docs/audit-trail.md`, and `specs/spec-v54.md` updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (26 gates; wiring 30 modules / 582 entries; corpus 886 / dims 889 / derivation 582/582 / citation-coverage 582; check-readme-counts agrees 582 / 608), `npm test` (**5,519 unit tests**, +1), `npm run build` (582 tile + 24 group shells, 608-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile` (608 shells, zero horizontal scroll at 320 px), and the render-no-nan + a11y Chromium checks on the new tile all green.

### chore(docs): re-audit v43/v44/v47/v50 tiles + add README lazy-load sequence diagram; stamps 0.48.3, 2026-06-12

A first-principles correctness re-audit plus one new architecture diagram. No code, data, tile, or shipped-output change (catalog stays 581).

- **v43/v44/v47/v50 correctness re-audit.** Hand-derived the new-math tiles from spec-v42 through spec-v51 (the gap between the prior v37-v41 audit and the in-session v52/v53 tiles): `tank-volume` horizontal-cylinder segment area A=R^2 acos((R-h)/R)-(R-h)sqrt(2Rh-h^2) (D24/L48/h12 -> 50% half-full, verified at h=R and h=2R); `circular-arc` R=(chord^2/4+rise^2)/(2 rise) (24/4 -> R20, 73.74 deg); `circle-from-3-points` circumcenter determinant ((0,0)/(4,0)/(0,3) -> center (2,1.5), R2.5); `bakers-percentage` ingredient=flour*pct/100 (1000 g @65/2/1% -> 1680 g dough, 420 g/piece). All match; no fix needed. Four consecutive clean re-audits now confirm the catalog is correct, not merely gate-consistent.
- **README lazy-load sequence diagram.** Added a `sequenceDiagram` to the System-design section showing the dynamic tile-open path (ensureTools lazy import, renderToolView building the shell synchronously, the three parallel cached dynamic imports of citations.js / the calc group module / the support libs, the crash-safe try/catch render boundary, and the service-worker warm path). Complements the existing value-flow and static-component diagrams, which never sequenced the lazy-load / memoization / crash-safe design the prose describes. Verified accurate against `app.js`.
- **Verified.** `npm run build` + `npm run lint` (26 gates) + `npm test` (**5,518 unit tests**) + `npm run audit` (6 stages) all green.

### feat: spec-v53 (Linear Interpolation) -- 1 tile in calc-cross.js, catalog 580 -> 581; stamps 0.48.0, 2026-06-12

A single first-principles, hand-verifiable tile deepening Group G (Cross-Trade Utilities) with the most universal chart-reading operation in the trades. No new group, no new module, no new dependencies. Lands in `calc-cross.js`.

- **G (Cross-Trade) +1.** `linear-interpolation` -- Linear Interpolation. Read a value between two known table/chart points: `y = y1 + (x - x1) x (y2 - y1) / (x2 - x1)`, with the slope and an extrapolation flag when the query falls outside the two reference points. The operation appears *inside* a dozen tiles already (NEMA derate rows, ACI w/c curves, refrigerant tables, livestock-water tables) -- that internal ubiquity is the evidence the standalone gap is real. Worked example: (0,10) and (10,30) at x=4 -> y=18, slope 2 (within range); x=15 against (0,0)-(10,100) -> y=150 flagged as extrapolation; descending x order handled. Equal x-values (vertical line) and non-finite inputs return an error.
- **Module / cap.** Lands in `calc-cross.js` (93.4% of its 36 KB cap; no bump). The `tools-data.js` registry crossed its cap on the new row, so its gzip cap was bumped 48000 -> 50000 B (lazy-loaded, not in the home-view payload). The new row is in the spec-v43 Group-G appendix, so the Group-G original-block count assertion is unaffected.
- **Correctness re-audit.** This change also lands an independent re-derivation of the spec-v40 calc-shop batch (10 tiles): every formula and empirical constant (machining-time, turning/drilling MRR, surface finish, taper, three-wire 0.57735/1.51553, punch force, press-brake 575, weld duty cycle, IIW carbon equivalent) was re-checked from first principles and all match -- the recent catalog additions are confirmed correct, not merely self-consistent. No correction needed.
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json`, `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (5), the `app.js` `CROSS_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotation, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (every gate; wiring lint **30 renderer modules / 581 tile-id entries**; check-readme-counts agrees 581 / 607; corpus 885, dimensions 888, derivation/source 581/581), `npm test` (**5,518 unit tests**), `npm run build` (581 tile + 24 group shells, 607-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify` (123), and a browser read of the rendered example output to the digit (y 18, slope 2, zero console errors).

### feat: spec-v52 (Hiking Time, Naismith's Rule) -- 1 tile in calc-field.js, catalog 579 -> 580; stamps 0.47.0, 2026-06-12

A single first-principles, hand-verifiable tile deepening Group P (Field, Backcountry, and SAR). No new group, no new module, no new dependencies. Lands in `calc-field.js`.

- **P (Field/SAR) +1.** `hiking-time` -- Hiking Time (Naismith's Rule). The canonical backcountry / SAR trip-time estimate: flat-ground time (horizontal distance / pace) plus an ascent penalty of one hour per 600 m (about 2000 ft) of climb, scaled by a terrain / fatigue factor, in hours and h:min with the flat-and-ascent breakdown. Distance in km or mi, ascent in m or ft; default pace 5 km/h (3 mph). Group P had pace-count distance, navigation, and SAR detection but no time-over-distance-and-terrain estimate. Concept-check: distinct from `pacing-distance` (steps to distance, not time). Worked example: 10 km / 600 m ascent / 5 km/h -> 2 hr flat + 1 hr ascent = 3 h 0 min; a 1.5x factor -> 4 h 30 min; 6 mi / 2000 ft / default 3 mph -> ~3 h 1 min. Non-positive distance, negative ascent, and non-finite inputs return an error; a blank pace defaults rather than dividing by zero.
- **Module / cap.** Lands in `calc-field.js` (89.8% -> 96.8% of its 22 KB cap; no bump, but the next Group-P tile needs one). `tools-data.js` fit the new row within its 48000 B cap (now 98%). The new row is in the spec-v25 Group-P appendix, so the Group-P original-block citation count assertion is unaffected.
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json`, `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (5), the `app.js` `FIELD_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotation, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (every gate; wiring lint **30 renderer modules / 580 tile-id entries**; the spec-v49 check-readme-counts gate agrees at 580 / 606; corpus 884, dimensions 887, derivation/source 580/580), `npm test` (**5,517 unit tests**), `npm run build` (580 tile + 24 group shells, 606-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify` (123), and a browser read of the rendered example output to the digit (3 h 0 min; flat 2.00 hr + ascent 1.00 hr, zero console errors).

### feat: spec-v51 (Stage Lighting Beam and Throw) -- 1 tile in calc-stage.js, catalog 578 -> 579; stamps 0.46.0, 2026-06-12

A single first-principles, hand-verifiable tile deepening Group N (Stage and Live Production) with the one production pillar it lacked: lighting. No new group, no new module, no new dependencies. Lands in `calc-stage.js`.

- **N (Stage) +1.** `lighting-beam` -- Stage Lighting Beam and Throw. Theatrical fixture photometry: the beam (pool) diameter at a throw distance (`2 x throw x tan(beam angle / 2)`) and the center-beam illuminance by the inverse-square law (`E = candela / distance^2`, or candela estimated from a fixture's total lumens spread over the beam cone), in footcandles and lux. Group N already covered audio (SPL / dB / impedance / amp / time-alignment), rigging (truss / rigging-check), power (distro / neutral-imbalance), and control (DMX); lighting was the missing fourth department. Concept-check: distinct from the existing architectural `lux-to-footcandle` lumen-method tile (a room area-average from a flux budget) -- this is a single aimed fixture, the point-source model manufacturers publish photometric cut sheets in. Worked example: 20-degree fixture, 30 ft throw, 100,000 cd -> 10.58 ft pool, 111.1 fc / 1196 lux; double the throw and the illuminance quarters. Beam angle outside (0,180), non-positive throw / intensity, and non-finite inputs return an error.
- **Module / cap.** Lands in `calc-stage.js` (crossed its 17 KB cap on the new tile -> bumped 17 -> 18.5 KB; lazy-loaded). `tools-data.js` fit the new row within its 48000 B cap. The new row is in the spec-v24 Group-N appendix, so the Group-N original-block citation count assertion is unaffected.
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json`, `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (5), the `app.js` `STAGE_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotation, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (every gate; wiring lint **30 renderer modules / 579 tile-id entries**; the spec-v49 check-readme-counts gate agrees at 579 / 605; corpus 883, dimensions 886, derivation/source 579/579), `npm test` (**5,516 unit tests**), `npm run build` (579 tile + 24 group shells, 605-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify` (123), and a browser read of the rendered example output to the digit (10.58 ft / 3.22 m beam, 111.1 fc / 1196 lux, zero console errors).

### feat: spec-v50 (Baker's Percentage) -- 1 tile in calc-kitchen.js, catalog 577 -> 578; stamps 0.45.0, 2026-06-12

A single first-principles, hand-verifiable tile deepening Group O (Kitchen and Food Service) -- at 7 tiles the most under-served substantive group. No new group, no new module, no new dependencies. Lands in `calc-kitchen.js`.

- **O (Kitchen) +1.** `bakers-percentage` -- Baker's Percentage / Dough Hydration. The bakery / pizzeria formulation method: flour = 100%, every ingredient is its weight as a percent of flour, hydration = water / flour. From the flour weight and the percentages it gives each ingredient weight, total dough weight, total formula percent, and per-piece weight (grams, with an ounce conversion on totals). A concept-check against the 577 live tiles found this is the one common kitchen formulation `recipe-scale` explicitly does **not** handle -- its renderer warns "bakers' percentages do not scale linearly" -- and it is distinct from `recipe-scale` (factor scaling) and `yield-ep` (AP/EP yield). Worked example: 1000 g flour at 65% hydration / 2% salt / 1% yeast over 4 pieces -> 650 g water, 20 g salt, 10 g yeast, 1680 g dough, 168% total formula, 420 g per piece. Non-positive flour, negative percentages, and non-finite inputs return an error; with no piece count the per-piece field is null (not a leak).
- **Module / cap.** Lands in `calc-kitchen.js` (~84% -> ~94% of its 13 KB cap; no bump, but the next kitchen tile needs one). `tools-data.js` fit the new row within its 48000 B cap.
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json`, `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (5), the `app.js` `KITCHEN_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotation, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block, the Group-O count assertion in `citations.test.js` (7 -> 8), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (every gate; wiring lint **30 renderer modules / 578 tile-id entries**; the spec-v49 check-readme-counts gate agrees at 578 / 604; corpus 882, dimensions 885, derivation/source 578/578), `npm test` (**5,515 unit tests**), `npm run build` (578 tile + 24 group shells, 604-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify` (123), and a browser read of the rendered example output to the digit (650 g water / 1680 g dough / 420 g per piece, zero console errors).

### chore: spec-v49 (README catalog-count gate) -- fix 3 stale Mermaid-diagram counts + new check-readme-counts gate; stamps 0.44.2, 2026-06-12

A doc-accuracy fix plus the gate that prevents its recurrence (the v45/v46/v48 claim-audit lineage). No tiles, no calculator changes, no shipped-output change (catalog stays 577).

- **The finding.** Two README Mermaid diagrams had silently-stale counts. The per-spec count-bump recipe edits prose, the cheat-sheet, and (since a few sessions ago) the correctness-pipeline diagram explicitly -- because Mermaid glues the number to a literal `\n` (`corpus row\n881`) and a `\b<old>\b` substitution does not match a digit glued to the `n` of `\n`. But the **architecture** diagram (`calc-*.js\n28 group modules`) and the **prerendered-shell** diagram (`\n555 static shells`, `sitemap.xml\n581 URLs`) were never on that explicit-edit list, so they drifted across ~20 spec landings while the prose beside them stayed correct (live: 30 modules, 577 tiles, 603 URLs).
- **The fix.** Corrected the three nodes (28 -> 30, 555 -> 577, 581 -> 603). The Phase F node (`390 tests / 66 monotonicity batches`) was checked and is current.
- **The gate.** `scripts/check-readme-counts.mjs` (wired into `npm run lint` after `check-discoverability`; deterministic, offline) derives live counts (tiles from `tools-data.js`, modules from the `calc-*.js` glob, groups from distinct `group:` letters, sitemap = tiles + groups + 2) and asserts every **label-anchored** count claim in the README matches -- anchoring on the descriptive label (`static shells`, `group modules`, `URLs`, `shell per tile (N)`) so it catches drift in prose or a Mermaid node alike, while not false-matching the bare number in historical "576 -> 577" stanzas. Negative-tested (re-introducing any stale number reddens it). Lint chain: 25 -> **26 gates**.
- **Docs.** README lint-chain count (25 -> 26) + a `check-readme-counts` row; `check:readme-counts` npm alias; spec-range to v49.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (26 gates green incl. check-readme-counts), `npm test` (**5,514 unit tests**, unchanged), `npm run data:verify` (123).

### chore: spec-v48 (Content-Security-Policy integrity gate) -- new check-csp lint gate; stamps 0.44.1, 2026-06-12

A security-invariant gate in the lineage of spec-v45 / spec-v46. No tiles, no calculator changes, no change to shipped output (catalog stays 577); it adds a lint gate that protects the CSP.

- **The finding.** The "0 trackers / 0 LLM calls / works offline" promise is enforced at runtime by a tight CSP (`default-src 'self'`, `script-src 'self' 'sha256-...'`, `connect-src 'self'`, `object-src 'none'`), shipped twice -- the `<meta>` in `index.html` and the `Content-Security-Policy` header in `_headers`. The single inline boot script is pinned by sha256, and the comment above it spells out a manual two-place maintenance step ("recompute its sha256 and update BOTH") that **nothing enforced**. Two silent failure modes: hash drift (edit the boot script, forget the hash -> the `<meta>` CSP blocks it with only a theme-flash symptom, and a drift in `_headers` is never exercised by the local suite, so it ships silently); and posture weakening (relax `script-src`/`connect-src` to admit a CDN or API).
- **The gate.** `scripts/check-csp.mjs` (wired into `npm run lint` after `check-manifests`; deterministic, offline) strips HTML comments (one literally contains `<script>`), extracts the single bare inline boot script, recomputes its sha256, and asserts the hash appears in `script-src` in **both** files; that `script-src` is exactly `'self'` + the hash (no host, no `unsafe-*`); that `default-src`/`connect-src` are exactly `'self'` and `object-src` is `'none'`; and that **no directive in either CSP admits an external origin**. Negative-tested: hash drift, an external `connect-src` origin, and a weakened directive each redden it. Currently green on the live repo (`sha256-0qFL...` matches both copies).
- **Docs.** Added the `check-csp` row to the README lint-chain table; corrected the lint-chain count (the README disagreed with itself -- "23-gate" vs "24 gates" -- now **25**); the safety section notes the CSP is now build-time gated. `npm run check:csp` alias added.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (25 gates, all green incl. check-csp), `npm test` (**5,514 unit tests**, unchanged), `npm run data:verify` (123).

### feat: spec-v47 (Circle Through Three Points) -- 1 tile in calc-fab.js, catalog 576 -> 577; stamps 0.44.0, 2026-06-12

A single first-principles, hand-verifiable tile deepening Group G (Cross-Trade Utilities). No new group, no new module, no new dependencies. Lands in `calc-fab.js` (the Group G fabrication & layout bench).

- **G (Cross-Trade) +1.** `circle-from-3-points` -- Circle Through Three Points. The inverse of `bolt-circle`: recover a circle's center and radius from three measured points on its arc (the triangle's circumcircle), for when the chord and a midspan rise cannot be measured directly but three points off the curve can. Center = circumcenter (`D = 2(x1(y2-y3)+x2(y3-y1)+x3(y1-y2))`, then the standard circumcenter coordinates), radius = distance from the center to any point; diameter and circumference follow. First-principles coordinate geometry, hand-verified: points (0,0), (4,0), (0,3) -> center (2, 1.5), radius 2.5 (a right triangle's hypotenuse is the diameter); (5,0),(0,5),(-5,0) -> center (0,0), radius 5. Collinear or coincident points (zero determinant) and non-finite inputs return an error. A concept-check against the 576 live tiles found `bolt-circle` lays out holes on a known circle and `circular-arc` uses a chord + midspan rise, but no tile recovered the circle from three arbitrary points.
- **Module / cap.** Lands in `calc-fab.js` (~84% -> ~86% of its 20 KB cap; no bump). `tools-data.js` fit the new row within its 48000 B cap.
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json`, `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (5), the `app.js` `FAB_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotation, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block pinning the worked example + the R5 case + collinear / coincident / non-finite seams, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (every gate; wiring lint **30 renderer modules / 577 tile-id entries**; corpus 881, dimensions 884, derivation/source 577/577), `npm test` (**5,514 unit tests**), `npm run build` (577 tile + 24 group shells, 603-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify` (123), `check:dist` + `check:shells` + `check:shell-mobile`, and a browser read of the rendered example output to the digit (center (2, 1.5), radius 2.5, diameter/circumference 5 / 15.7080, zero console errors).

### chore: spec-v46 (dist/ integrity gate activation) -- wire the dormant check-dist gate into CI; rides the 0.44.0 stamp, 2026-06-12

Housekeeping in the spirit of spec-v45. No tiles, no calculator changes. A dormant-gate audit (diff `scripts/check-*.mjs` against the gates referenced in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` and the `npm run lint` chain) found that `check-dist` (the spec-v12 G.3 dist/-vs-runtime cross-check -- every same-origin reference in a shipped `dist/` file must resolve to a file present in `dist/`, catching a dangling `fetch`/asset reference the build did not copy) was **never invoked by CI** (only `check-shell-mobile` and, as of v45, `check-shells` ran in the build-having job). It is a deterministic, offline gate, so v46 wires `npm run check:dist` into the integration job after `npm run build`. It currently resolves **2,011 same-origin references across 790 dist/ files with zero dangling**, so wiring it is safe and locks that in. (`check-free-access` remains intentionally opt-in -- it probes external URLs over the network and should not block a release.)

### feat: spec-v45 (prerendered shell citation + gate activation) -- formula/source in every tile shell, check-shells wired into CI; stamps 0.43.0, 2026-06-11

A public-surface / SEO enhancement to the spec-v13 prerendered static shells. No new tiles, no calculator changes (catalog stays 576); the SPA is byte-for-byte unchanged.

- **Reference content in the shells.** Until now each static `/tools/<id>/index.html` shell carried the tile name, lead, related tiles, an Audience block, and a Posture block -- but **not the formula or the citation**, i.e. not the reference content the prerender exists to expose (README: "the cited reference content of 576 tiles would otherwise be invisible to general web search"). `build-shells.mjs` now imports `CITATIONS` and emits a **Formula and source** section in every tile shell (the `formula` and `edition` fields, HTML-escaped), placed after the "Run the calculator" link. A reader landing on `/tools/ohms-law/` from a search now sees `V = I * R` and its authority in static, zero-JavaScript HTML. Every tile has a `CITATIONS` entry (v19/v22 coverage), so every shell carries it; adds ~0.3-0.4 KB gz (largest tile shell ~2.3 KB of the 6 KB cap).
- **Activated a dormant gate.** `scripts/check-shells.mjs` (the spec-v13 content gate: title/description caps, canonical, Open Graph / Twitter, JSON-LD allowlist, gzip budgets, banned words) had a unit-test mirror but was **never invoked by `.github/workflows/ci.yml`** -- only `check-shell-mobile` ran, so the shell content contract was unenforced on push. v45 adds the `npm run check:shells` step to the integration job (which already builds `dist/`). The gate also gained a per-tile assertion that the formula/source block is present; a negative test confirms it fails when the section is removed.
- **Docs.** Corrected the README "Discoverable surface" shell description (it had claimed an inline notice + worked example that were never in the shell) to match reality (formula/source + Audience + related tiles + Posture), updated the CI section to list `check-shells`, and added the Formula-and-source block to the `docs/seo.md` shell-content model.
- **Verified.** `npm run build` + `npm run check:shells` (576 tile + 24 group shells green, every tile shell carrying the block), `npm run check:shell-mobile` (602 routes, zero horizontal scroll at 320 px with the new text), `npm run lint`, `npm test` (**5,513 unit tests**, unchanged), `npm run data:verify` (123), and a browser read of an enriched shell.

### feat: spec-v44 (Circular Arc Layout) -- 1 tile in calc-fab.js, catalog 575 -> 576; stamps 0.42.0, 2026-06-11

A single first-principles, hand-verifiable tile deepening Group G (Cross-Trade Utilities). No new group, no new module, no new dependencies, no telemetry, no AI, US standards only. Lands in `calc-fab.js` (the Group G fabrication & layout bench, ~79% of cap).

- **G (Cross-Trade) +1.** `circular-arc` -- Circular Arc Layout. Recovers the radius, arc length, and central angle of a circular arc from a measured chord (span) and rise (sagitta / middle ordinate) at midspan: `R = (chord^2/4 + rise^2)/(2 x rise)`, central angle `= 2 x acos((R - rise)/R)` (valid for minor and major arcs), arc length `= R x angle`. The everyday field-layout question for an arch, curved trim / handrail, sheet-metal radius, or road curve. A concept-check against the 575 live tiles found `bolt-circle` lays out hole positions on a known circle but no tile recovers a curve's radius from a chord and rise. Worked example: chord 24 in, rise 4 in -> radius 20 in (diameter 40 in), central angle 73.7398 deg, arc 25.7400 in; a rise equal to the radius is a semicircle (chord 20 / rise 10 -> 180 deg, arc 31.4159 in); a rise greater than the radius is a major arc (chord 10 / rise 12 -> 269.5 deg). Non-positive chord / rise and non-finite inputs return an error.
- **Module / cap.** Lands in `calc-fab.js` (~79% -> ~81% of its 20 KB cap; no bump), the natural home alongside `bolt-circle` / `sine-bar` / `pipe-template-wrap` / the conduit-bend suite. `tools-data.js` fit the new row within its current 48000 B cap.
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json`, `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (5), the `app.js` `FAB_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotation, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block pinning the worked example + semicircle + major-arc + error seams, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index.
