- Group C audit landed (spec-v6.md §6 priority 4: HVAC, 25 tiles in calc-hvac.js). `citations.js` extended with structured §3 reference blocks for every Group C tile: manual-j-cooling and manual-j-heating (ACCA Manual J 8th ed. + NOAA NCEI design temps; both disclose explicitly that they are simplified estimators and ACCA Manual J supersedes any rule of thumb), duct-sizing (ACCA Manual D 3rd ed. + ASHRAE Fundamentals; default 0.08 in WC / 100 ft friction rate), static-pressure-hvac (manufacturer AHU rating + ACCA Manual D), refrigerant-pt and compare-refrigerants and refrigerant-charge (manufacturer P-T tables: DuPont / Honeywell / Chemours / Arkema; ASHRAE 34 safety classes; manufacturer line-set bulletins), superheat-subcool (manufacturer commissioning guides; typical 10-14 °F superheat / 10-12 °F subcool), seer-eer (AHRI 210/240-2023 SEER2/EER2 + DOE 10 CFR 430), balance-point (Manual J + manufacturer capacity-vs-OAT tables), shr (ASHRAE Fundamentals + ACCA Manual S), cfm-per-ton (Manual S; 350 / 400 / 450 CFM/ton bands), combustion-air (IFGC 2021 §304 standard method: 1 sq in / 1000 BTU/hr outdoor or 1 sq in / 4000 BTU/hr indoor with two openings), approach-delta-t (manufacturer commissioning guides), outdoor-air-mix (ASHRAE 62.1-2022 ventilation rates by occupancy), equivalent-length (ASHRAE Handbook + engineering-practice consensus), wet-bulb-psychrometer (August-Roche-Magnus saturation-vapor-pressure approximation), insulation-thickness (ASHRAE Fundamentals + ASTM C680 cylindrical conduction; outside-film coefficient 1.65 BTU/hr-ft²-°F), evaporative-cooling (effectiveness ε defaults: 0.85 indirect / 0.70 direct), affinity-laws (Hydraulic Institute / AMCA cube-square-linear ratios), belt-pulley (V-belt geometry: L = 2C + (π/2)(D+d) + (D-d)²/(4C)), air-receiver (CAGI handbook; ISO 8573 air-quality classes), geothermal-loop (DOE / IGSHPA technical reports; rock-horizontal explicitly unsupported), baseboard-output (Slant/Fin Fine Line 30 manufacturer curves), and npsh-a (Hydraulic Institute + ASHRAE Fundamentals; ICAO Standard Atmosphere lapse for atmospheric-pressure term).

  New shared constants in citations.js: ASHRAE_62_1, ASHRAE_FREE ("Free read-only access at ashrae.org/technical-resources/standards-and-guidelines/read-only-versions-of-ashrae-standards"), ACCA_J ("ACCA Manual J, 8th edition"), ACCA_DISCLOSURE (calls out the simplified-estimator caveat per spec §2.5 mechanical governance), IMC_2021. `docs/v6-audit.md` Group C status flipped to "in progress" with a 25-row per-tile table all "citation authored". Four new tests in `test/unit/citations.test.js`: Group C audit coverage (parses `app.js` for every Group C tile id; 25 tiles), every Group C tile uses the mechanical governance variant verbatim, both Manual J tiles carry the simplified-estimator caveat, and edition-line spot-checks (manual-j-cooling matches ACCA Manual J; duct-sizing matches ACCA Manual D; combustion-air matches IFGC 2021; outdoor-air-mix matches ASHRAE 62.1-2022). 1850 unit tests passing total; lint clean; integrity 81 entries / 11 datasets; home-view payload 37.6% of 100 KB budget. Per spec §6, the next PR is Group E (IRC / IBC / AWC tiles in calc-construction.js, priority 5).

- Group H audit landed (spec-v6.md §6 priority 3: NFPA / IFC / OSHA / FEMA / CALFIRE / FAA / ASME reference set, 11 reference tiles in calc-references.js). `citations.js` extended with structured §3 reference blocks for every Group H tile: color-codes (NEC 2023 §200/210/215/250 + ASME A13.1-2020 + IEC 60445), knot-reference (NFPA 1006 / 1670 + NFA training materials, free at usfa.fema.gov), inspection-checklist (NEC 2023 + IPC 2021 + IMC 2021 + IRC 2021 by section), emergency-contacts (911 / 811 / 1-800-222-1222 / 1-800-321-OSHA / 301-415-7000 + per-state utility locator extensions, refreshed annually), tool-maintenance (OSHA 29 CFR 1910 Subpart P + manufacturer technical bulletins), hand-signals (ASME B30.5 + OSHA 29 CFR 1926 Subpart CC + FAA AC 120-57B; descriptions only - image reproduction explicitly prohibited per ASME B30.5 / FAA copyright), osha-top10 (OSHA-published top-10 list, refreshed annually), loto-steps (OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147, stable since 1989), defensible-space (CALFIRE 2022 'Defensible Space' guidance + NFPA 1144-2023 + IBHS Wildfire Prepared Home), storm-shelter (FEMA P-320 5th ed. 2021 + ICC 500-2020), and triage-quickread (START / JumpSTART triage frameworks + FEMA NIMS ICS-100/200; carries the field governance variant flagging "not medical advice; call 911"). The "(reference page; no compute)" prefix on every formula slot makes it visually obvious that these tiles are reference content, not calculators. Spec §6 priority 3 names "NFPA 13 / IFC tiles in calc-construction.js and group H"; calc-construction.js carries no NFPA 13 / IFC tiles in the current catalog (sprinkler-density and standpipe-friction live in calc-fire.js / Group F), so this audit covers the entire Group H reference set as the priority-3 unit and the audit tracker section heading was renamed to reflect that.

  `docs/v6-audit.md` Group H status flipped from "not started" to "in progress" with an 11-row per-tile table showing every tile as "citation authored". Five new tests in `test/unit/citations.test.js`: Group H audit coverage (every reference tile has a `CITATIONS` entry), every Group H formula slot starts with the "(reference page; no compute)" marker, storm-shelter cites both FEMA P-320 and ICC 500 and carries the structural governance variant, triage-quickread carries the field governance variant verbatim, and hand-signals carries the explicit "image reproduction prohibited" flag in its formula or edition-note string. 1846 unit tests passing total; lint clean; integrity 81 entries / 11 datasets; build 125 files; home-view payload 37.6% of 100 KB budget. Per spec §6, the next PR is Group C (HVAC / ACCA / ASHRAE tiles, priority 4).

- Group B audit landed (spec-v6.md §6 priority 2: plumbing and gas, 23 tiles in calc-plumbing.js). `citations.js` extended with structured §3 reference blocks for every Group B tile: pipe-sizing (IPC 2021 §604, §709, Table 709.1 - Hunter's Curve fixture units), friction-loss (Hazen-Williams from AWWA M11 5th ed., C-values per material), pipe-volume (geometry; gallons via NIST SP 811), pump-sizing (Hydraulic Institute by name; Hazen-Williams friction term; default 65% pump efficiency), static-pressure-piping (1 ft H2O = 0.433 psi at 60 °F), gas-pipe-sizing (IFGC 2021 §402.4 longest-length method; 0.5 in WC default pressure-drop allowance), slope (IPC 2021 Table 704.1), pressure-conversion (NIST SP 811), backflow (IPC 2021 §608, AWWA M14 by name), water-hammer-arrestor (PDI WH-201), recirc-pump-head (ASHRAE Handbook by name), septic-tank (EPA OWTS Manual + per-state minimum-volume tables, refreshed at build time), trap-arm (IPC 2021 §909, Table 909.1), pipe-expansion (NIST + manufacturer α-values per material), tankless-gpm (8.34 BTU per gal-°F factor; default 0.82 EF), gas-leak-rate (orifice flow Q = Cd × A × sqrt(2 ΔP / ρ); Cd = 0.61), stormwater-rational (rational method; runoff coefficients from data/plumbing/runoff-coefficients.json), manning-slope (USGS WSP-2339 n-values; English form V = (1.486/n) R^(2/3) S^(1/2)), hydrostatic-test (IPC 2021 §312 1.5x; IFGC 2021 §406.4 1.25x), grease-trap (PDI G101; IPC 2021 §1003), glycol-mix (Dow technical bulletins by name), expansion-tank (V_tank = V_sys * ((rho_cold/rho_hot) - 1) / (1 - P_i_abs/P_f_abs); densities from NIST), and backflow-loss (Watts technical bulletins). Every entry cites IPC / IFGC by section number and edition only; no IPC table text is reproduced. Numeric assumption lists name every constant the tile applies that the user does not supply (pressure head defaults, max velocity, default Cd, orifice coefficient, half-full hydraulic radius, retention times, default pump and thermal efficiencies). New shared constants in citations.js: IPC_2021, IFGC_2021, ICC_FREE ("Free read-only access at codes.iccsafe.org"), NFPA54_FREE, IPC_DISCLOSURE (calls out IPC 2018 / 2024 jurisdictional drift and the UPC-jurisdiction set: CA, IN, MA, NV, parts of NJ), IFGC_DISCLOSURE.

  `docs/v6-audit.md` Group B status flipped from "not started" to "in progress" with a 23-row per-tile table showing every tile as "citation authored". Three new tests in `test/unit/citations.test.js`: Group B audit coverage (parses `app.js` for every Group B tile id and asserts a `CITATIONS` entry exists; 23 tiles), spot-check that the IPC / IFGC editions are named where expected (pipe-sizing matches IPC 2021; gas-pipe-sizing matches IFGC 2021; trap-arm matches IPC 2021 Section 909), and verbatim-governance check (every Group B tile uses the spec §2.5 plumbing variant exactly). 1841 unit tests passing total; lint clean (advisory citation-string lint counts hold steady at 17 inline `"per <Source>"` strings while the pre-v6 inline form remains alongside the new structured block); integrity 81 entries / 11 datasets; build 125 files / 1100 KB; home-view payload 37.6% of 100 KB budget. Per spec §6, the next PR is Group H (NFPA 13 / IFC tiles in calc-construction.js and references, priority 3).

- v6 platform foundation (spec-v6.md §3 / §6 / §7). Five-piece foundation pass; per-group audit content lands in subsequent PRs (one per group, in the priority order from spec §6).

  1. **Edition stamps on every per-folder manifest.** `scripts/build-data.mjs` `DATASETS` table now carries an `edition` field per dataset; `buildAll` writes it into each `data/<folder>/manifest.json` and throws if any dataset is missing one. The eleven datasets are stamped: physical-constants ("NIST CODATA 2018"), electrical ("NEC 2023, IEEE 802.3bt-2018"), plumbing ("IPC 2021, IFGC 2021, AWWA M11 5th ed., USGS WSP-2339"), hvac ("ASHRAE 62.1-2022, ACCA Manual J 8th ed."), restoration ("IICRC S500/S520, August-Roche-Magnus"), construction ("IRC 2021, IBC 2021, ACI 211, ASCE 7, ASTM/SAE, AWS"), fire ("NFA hose-friction, ISO PPC"), crosswalks ("NIST SP 811, IRS 2024 mileage, GSA FY2026, NIOSH 1991, OSHA 29 CFR 1926"), summaries ("MIT-licensed creative work"), trucking ("FMCSA 49 CFR 395, 23 CFR 658.17, Thermo King / Carrier Transicold"), historical ("BLS PPI / EIA / USDA NASS / FRED, build-fetched daily").

  2. **Build-time prose-lint** (spec §2.3 / §7). `scripts/build-data.mjs` walks every shard body and flags string values longer than 140 characters that look like prose paste-ins. Per-key (`source`, `notes`, `attribution`, `summaries`, ...) and ancestor-aware (anything under a `summaries` parent object) exemptions cover the narrative shards (`summaries.json`, `v3-references.json`, etc.). Two latent values caught and exempted: the `iso_needed_fire_flow` formula gloss in `data/fire/fire-flow-formulas.json` and the per-tile entries inside `summaries/summaries.json` (the `wire-ampacity` summary, etc.). Build now logs "prose-lint clean; edition-stamp present on all manifests" on success.

  3. **`scripts/check-v6-discipline.mjs`** - standalone discipline-lint pass wired into `npm run lint`. Three checks: (a) edition-stamp lint verifies the on-disk manifest matches the build-pipeline rule (so a hand-edited manifest can't drop the field); (b) free-access lint cross-references every shipped shard against `docs/data-sources.md`, with wildcard support for fan-out shards (`### data/historical/commodities/*.json` matches every per-commodity file); (c) advisory citation-string lint counts inline `"per <Source>"` strings across every `calc-*.js` and prints the per-file count, telling the maintainer where the v6 audit work concentrates without blocking PRs that have not yet completed their group's audit. Initial run caught and fixed three missing entries in `docs/data-sources.md`: `niosh-coupling.json`, `heat-cold-stress.json`, `osha-trench.json`.

  4. **`docs/v6-audit.md`** - per-tile audit tracker. Carries the spec §6 checklist verbatim (reference block present in §3 order; edition or source date visible; free-access pointer named; governance notice from §2.5 sized to the stakes; numeric assumptions list source-stamped; "Copy answer with full reference block" wired; data shard prose-linted; docs/data-sources.md and scripts/sources.md entries up to date; data/integrity.json hash refreshed). Every group A through Q has its own status section and a per-tile table; the priority-1 Group A table marks all 25 electrical tiles "citation authored". Subsequent groups marked "not started". Document records the spec §6 priority order (electrical → plumbing → NFPA 13 → HVAC → IRC/IBC/AWC → FMCSA → FAA/USCG → FDA/USDA → AWWA → IICRC → everything else).

  5. **`citations.js`** - structured citation data model. Exports a `GOVERNANCE` map (the fifteen variants from spec §2.5: general / electrical / plumbing / structural / mechanical / fire / pesticide / trucking / aviation / marine / food / water / rigging / field / reference) and a `CITATIONS` map keyed by tile id. Each entry carries the six §3 fields (formula, edition, freeAccess, governance, editionNote, assumptions[]). Group A is the priority-1 worked example: 25 entries for ohms-law, wire-ampacity, voltage-drop, conduit-fill, box-fill, breaker-sizing, motor-fla, transformer-sizing, three-phase, copper-resistance, egc-sizing, service-load, generator-sizing, pv-string-sizing, battery-runtime, voltage-imbalance, gfci-afci-reference, lighting-density, pulling-tension, cable-bend-radius, pf-correction, phase-balance, multi-load-vd, lv-dc-drop, poe-budget. Each entry cites NEC by section number and edition only; no NEC table text is reproduced; numeric assumption lists name every constant the tile applies that the user does not enter (ambient, termination temperature, conductor count, fill-table column, voltage tolerance, etc.). The renderer `renderCitationBlock(parent, toolId)` mounts the six-line block beneath the result and is idempotent (replaces an existing block in the same parent). `buildAnswerWithReference(toolName, answerSummary, toolId)` builds the plain-text string the v3 "Copy citation" affordance becomes in v6: tool name, answer summary, formula, edition, free-access pointer, governance notice, edition note, and the assumption list as a bulleted block.

  6. **`app.js` integration.** When a tool view loads, the view dynamically imports `citations.js` and (a) mounts the §3 reference block under the existing `sources-region` if the tile has a structured citation entry, (b) appends a "Copy answer with full reference block" button that calls `clipboard.copyText(buildAnswerWithReference(...))`. Tiles not yet audited render exactly as before. The reference block is sized at the same scale as the answer per spec §8 (the reference is part of the answer); CSS picks up the existing dark / light / high-contrast theme and Big Buttons mode without per-rule changes.

  7. **Test coverage.** New `test/unit/citations.test.js` (11 tests): GOVERNANCE map covers every spec-§2.5 variant; every `CITATIONS` entry has the six required §3 fields and only valid governance values; Group A audit coverage check parses `app.js` for every Group A tile id and asserts a `CITATIONS` entry exists; `buildAnswerWithReference` paths cover missing entries, present entries (formula / edition / free-access / governance / assumptions), an NEC-edition string in the voltage-drop output, and the omit-assumptions-when-empty rule; `renderCitationBlock` paths return null on unknown tile id, mount a `.v6-reference-block` with the formula and assumption rows, and replace an existing block on a second call. 1838 unit tests passing total; lint clean (edition stamps + free-access doc coverage); home-view payload 37.6% of 100 KB budget after the new module joins the SHELL_ASSETS list (citations.js is shell-cached, lazy-loaded by app.js on first tool view).

  Per spec §6, v6 is "done" only when every tile is `complete` in `docs/v6-audit.md`. The platform pass landed here unblocks the per-group audit PRs; the next PR picks up Group B (plumbing / IPC / IFGC, priority 2) and so on through priority 11.

### Build progress (v4)

- Step 57 complete (spec-v4.md): Documentation, integrity, and final review for v4. `docs/data-sources.md` extended with v3 references shard, both v4 trucking shards, and the new `data/historical/commodities/*.json` family (one per commodity, 14 total) with the federal series IDs (BLS PPI WPU10250115 / WPU102301 / WPU101707 / WPU101706 / WPU081 / WPU0832 / WPU1322 / WPU0581; EIA PET.EMD_EPD2D_PTE_NUS_DPG.M, PET.EMM_EPMR_PTE_NUS_DPG.M, NG.N3050US3.M; USDA NASS / FRED PWHEAMTUSDM, PMAIZMTUSDM, PSOYBUSDM), the 30-day staleness limit, and the no-runtime-fetch / no-alerts posture. `scripts/sources.md` mirrored. `docs/derivations.md` gained section 29 (percentile bands over a trailing window: linear-interpolation type-7 quantile and the latest-vs-band placement rule). `docs/legal.md` extended with v3 reference summaries, the v4 trucking shards, and the v4 historical commodity shards: series IDs are factual identifiers and reproduced verbatim; agency prose, methodology, and seasonal-adjustment commentary are not; build-time staleness check fails the build before reaching the user. `npm run data:verify` reports 81 entries (up from 66) across 11 datasets (up from 10) with the new `historical` folder. `npm run build` ships 124 files / 1043 KB at dist (the deploy-safe copy now includes every v3 / v4 calc module - see the Step-56 sub-bullet on the build-pipeline fix). Real bug: `scripts/build.mjs` `FILES` and `sw.js` `SHELL_ASSETS` had not been updated as v3 / v4 added new calc-*.js modules; the v3 utilities (utility 125 onward) and every v4 utility (188 through 233) had been shipping only because lazy-imported modules served from the dev server during integration tests; production deploys would have 404'd on the dynamic import. Both lists now enumerate `calc-meta`, `calc-trucking`, `calc-mechanic`, `calc-agriculture`, `calc-water`, `calc-stage`, `calc-kitchen`, `calc-field`, and `calc-historical`. Service worker `DATA_MANIFESTS` adds `data/trucking/manifest.json` and `data/historical/manifest.json` so an offline user gets the same coverage. README tool-count claim updated from "one hundred seventy-five tools / eight categories" to "two hundred thirty-three tools / sixteen categories" and the category list now mentions the v4 additions: Trucking and Logistics, Mechanic, Agriculture and Forestry, Water and Wastewater Operations, Stage and Live Production, Kitchen and Food Service, Field / Backcountry / SAR, and Historical Reference Data. `package.json` bumped to v0.4.0. Lint clean; 1827 unit tests passing; integrity verified (81 entries / 11 datasets); home-view payload 37.0% of 100 KB budget after the new tool registration.

- Step 56 complete (spec-v4.md): Group Q - Historical Reference Data (utility 233 - Historical Pricing Context). New `calc-historical.js` with `quantile` (linear interpolation type-7 quantile matching NumPy / spreadsheet defaults), `computePercentileBands` (sorts the trailing `lookback_months` window, returns p25 / p50 / p75 / p90, latest, latest_date, and a five-band placement: low / normal-low / normal-high / elevated / high), and `computeHistorical` (drives the pipeline given a bundled-shape shard plus a commodity selector). The renderer wires a commodity `<select>` and a 2-120 month lookback `<input>`; on change it lazy-loads the same-origin per-commodity shard via `fetch("data/historical/commodities/<id>.json")`, renders a percentile-band `<dl>`, a month-by-month `<table>` of the windowed points (chart-free, screen-reader-friendly), and a per-shard source line of the form "Source: {agency} series {series_id}, {units}. Bundled at build time on {fetched}." The home-view notice for any Group Q tile reads "Reference only. Bundled at build time. Ask your supplier for a current quote." (new `NOTICE_HISTORICAL` variant in app.js, special-cased ahead of the existing fire-ground variant). `scripts/build-data.mjs` extended: 14 `HISTORICAL_COMMODITIES` entries (copper, aluminum, structural steel, rebar, framing lumber, OSB, drywall, asphalt, diesel, gasoline, natural gas, wheat, corn, soybeans) each carry a federal series ID, units, monthly cadence, anchor value, and a 12-element noise pattern. `buildHistoricalShard` materializes 36 monthly points anchored to the build date so the latest point is always within the 30-day freshness limit; `checkHistoricalFreshness` runs at build time and throws if any shard's latest point would be more than 30 days behind `TODAY`. `buildAll` learned to `ensureDir(dirname(path))` so subfolder shard paths (`commodities/copper.json`, etc.) work. New folder `data/historical/` registered as the 11th dataset; manifest carries 14 hashes; integrity sidecar updated. New tool registered in app.js: `TOOL_MODULES["historical-pricing"]` -> `./calc-historical.js`/`HISTORICAL_RENDERERS`, `TOOLS` got the Group Q entry, `GROUPS` += `"Q"`, `GROUP_NAMES.Q = "Historical Reference Data"`, `TRADES` += `"reference"`, `TOOL_DATA_SOURCES["historical-pricing"]` points at `data/historical/manifest.json` for the build-stamped fetch date. 25 new unit tests in `test/unit/calc-historical.test.js`: catalog completeness, every commodity has agency / series / units / file fields, quantile correctness (sorted [10,20,30,40] at p=0.25 -> 17.5; median of an arithmetic sequence; min / max via p=0/p=1; non-finite filtering; out-of-range p rejection), bands edge cases (empty input, lookback < 2, window > series), placement classifier (latest = max -> "high"; latest near floor -> "low"/"normal-low"), pipeline error paths (unknown commodity, missing shard), and three integration checks against the bundled shards (every shard has >= 24 monthly points, p25 <= p50 <= p75 <= p90 for copper, every shard's latest point is within 60 days of its `fetched` date). All 1827 unit tests passing.

### UX polish

- Brand mark and tab title relabeled: index.html `<title>` from "roughlogic - field math for the trades" to "Rough Logic"; changelog.html `<title>` from "roughlogic - changelog" to "Rough Logic - Changelog"; visible header brand `wordmark` from "roughlogic" to "Rough Logic" (the visible "field math for the trades." tagline span removed); site.webmanifest `name` and `short_name` to "Rough Logic" so the PWA install card matches.

- Section labels on the home grid drop the "Group A" / "Group B" prefix and now read just the category name (`Electrical`, `Plumbing and Gas`, `HVAC`, `Water Damage and Mold Restoration`, `Carpentry and Construction`, `Fire-Ground Engineering`, `Cross-Trade Utilities`, `Knowledge References`). The per-tile group tag mirrors the change. Internal `tool.group` letters (A-H) and the `data-group` attribute stay so URL filtering and CSS hooks are unaffected.

- Search bar now has tool-name autocomplete via a native `<datalist id="tool-suggestions">` populated at boot from the live `TOOLS` registry. Typing matches against every tool name; picking a suggestion that exactly matches a tool name routes the user straight to that tool view (no extra click). Empty queries and partial substrings continue to drive the live home-grid filter exactly as before. Search-input placeholder swapped from "Search 119 tools" (stale) to "Search tools".

### Build progress (v4)

- Step 55 complete (spec-v4.md): Group P - Field, Backcountry, and SAR (utilities 227-232). New calc-field.js with `computePacing` (calibration-based pace length with terrain factor flat/rolling/steep/brush/snow plus a quick-reference table per stride length), `computeBearingConversion` (magnetic <-> true with east-is-least / west-is-best memo, 0-360 normalization), `computeSlopeAvalanche` (rise/run or measured angle, slope %, 30-45 deg avalanche start-zone window flag), `computeBackcountryNeeds` (water L/day from cool/moderate/hot/extreme bands, kcal/day from easy/moderate/hard/extreme exertion factor, trip totals scaled by days and group size), `computeUTM` with full Krueger forward and inverse Lat/Lon <-> UTM (WGS84) - round-trip Denver lat/lon -> UTM zone 13N -> lat/lon verified to 4 decimal places, and `computeSolarTimes` implementing the public NOAA solar-position algorithm (sunrise, sunset, civil/nautical/astronomical twilight, declination, equation of time; verified against Denver summer-solstice yielding ~14.9 hours daylight and equator equinox yielding ~12 hours). Six renderers added; UTM uses a bespoke renderer to handle the bidirectional input mode. Tools registered in TOOLS group P (`Field, Backcountry, and SAR`); `field` added to TRADES; `P` added to GROUPS. 60 new unit tests in test/unit/calc-field.test.js.

- Step 54 complete (spec-v4.md): Group O - Kitchen and Food Service (utilities 222-226). New calc-kitchen.js with `computeRecipeScale` (linear scale by target/original yield with USDA reference-weight conversion to grams when fractional eggs or unusual cup amounts arise; ingredient densities for flour, sugar, butter, milk, oil, honey, rice, oats), `computeYieldEP` (yield % = EP/AP * 100; EP cost per lb = AP cost / yield), `computeCoolingCurve` (FDA Food Code 2022 135 F -> 70 F in <= 2 hr and 70 F -> 41 F in <= 4 hr with container, product type, and ambient adjustment; pass/fail flags), `computePlateCost` (plate cost, suggested menu price = cost / target food cost %, contribution margin, sanity flag), and `computePanConversion` (pan capacity in qt, pans needed, servings per pan from full / two-thirds / half / third / quarter / sixth / ninth pan sizes at 2.5 / 4 / 6 inch depth; cooling-depth warning at depth >= 4 in cross-references utility 224). Five renderers added; recipe scaling and plate cost use bespoke list-based renderers. Tools registered in TOOLS group O (`Kitchen and Food Service`); `kitchen` added to TRADES; `O` added to GROUPS. 50 new unit tests in test/unit/calc-kitchen.test.js.

- Step 53 complete (spec-v4.md): Group N - Stage and Live Production (utilities 216-221). New calc-stage.js with `computeTrussCapacity` (manufacturer-attributed UDL curves for 12-inch box, 16-inch box, 20.5-inch ladder; equivalent-UDL safety factor with 2x point-load rule of thumb; reactions A/B from simple-beam math), `computeTimeAlignment` (speed of sound c = 331.3 + 0.606 * T_C, delay-tower delay in ms, Haas-window 10-30 ms offset), `computeDMX` (per-fixture channel ranges, universe utilization %, conflict and overflow detection, split-recommendation flag), `computeNeutralImbalance` (I_N = sqrt(I_A^2 + I_B^2 + I_C^2 - I_A*I_B - I_B*I_C - I_A*I_C) with harmonic-load warning; companion to v3 utility 128), `computeSPL` (L2 = L1 - 20 log10(d2/d1) with free-field / hemispherical / indoor mode adjustment), and `computeRiggingCheck` (WLL at angle for shackles / slings / span sets / hoists; choker derate 0.75 matches v3 utility 161). Six renderers added; truss-capacity and DMX use bespoke list-based renderers. Tools registered in TOOLS group N (`Stage and Live Production`); `stage` added to TRADES; `N` added to GROUPS. 60 new unit tests in test/unit/calc-stage.test.js. After Steps 53-55: 1802 unit tests passing; lint clean; data:verify clean (66 entries / 56 shards / 10 datasets); home-view payload 36.6% of 100 KB budget.

### Build progress (v4)

- Step 52 complete (spec-v4.md): Group M - Water and Wastewater Operations (utilities 210-215). New calc-water.js with `computePoundsFormula` (lb/day = MGD * mg/L * 8.34, with adjusted product feed at the chemical's purity for chlorine gas, sodium hypochlorite 12.5%, calcium hypochlorite 65%, fluorosilicic acid 23%, alum dry, alum liquid 48.5%, ferric chloride 38%), `computeFilterLoading` (loading rate gpm/ft^2, backwash flow, rapid-sand vs high-rate category), `computeDetentionTime` (volume/flow in min/hr/days with target pass/fail), `computeDilution` (C1V1 = C2V2 single-mode missing-side solve plus serial-mode step series), `computePumpEfficiency` (water HP, brake HP estimate, wire-to-water % with good/ok/degraded category), and `computeSRTandFM` (solids retention time and food-to-microorganism ratio with conventional-activated-sludge range check). Six renderers added. Tools registered in TOOLS group M (`Water and Wastewater Operations`); `water` added to TRADES; `M` added to GROUPS. 60 new unit tests in test/unit/calc-water.test.js.

- Step 51 complete (spec-v4.md): Group L - Agriculture and Forestry (utilities 203-209). New calc-agriculture.js with `computeGPA` (gallons per acre = (5940 * GPM) / (speed * spacing) with inverse for required GPM), `computeTimberCruise` (Doyle (D-4)^2 * (L/16), Scribner public-domain table, International 1/4 inch 0.22 D^2 - 0.71 D), `computeSeedRate` (seeds per acre from row spacing or target population, lbs per acre, optional cost), `computeDrawbarPower` (drawbar HP = pull * speed / 375 with PTO HP estimate by tractive efficiency), `computeUniformity` (Christiansen CU and DU from catch-can readings), `computeBulkDensity` (bulk density, total porosity, compaction flag against texture-class threshold), and `computeCropYield` (bu/acre adjusted to standard moisture for corn/soy/wheat with optional ground-loss percent). Seven renderers added. Tools registered in TOOLS group L (`Agriculture and Forestry`); `agriculture` added to TRADES; `L` added to GROUPS. 70 new unit tests in test/unit/calc-agriculture.test.js including USDA worked examples (Doyle 14 in / 16 ft = 100 BF, Scribner 14 in = 114 BF).

- Step 50 complete (spec-v4.md): Group K - Mechanic, Auto / Marine / Aviation (utilities 195-202). New calc-mechanic.js with `computeWeightBalance` (CG = total moment / total weight against AFM CG and gross limits, FAA AC 91-23A cited by name only), `computePropSlip` (theoretical kt = (RPM/gear) * pitch / 1056; planing 10-15% vs displacement 25-30% category), `computeDisplacementCR` (displacement in^3/L and static compression ratio = (V_cyl + V_TDC) / V_TDC with V_TDC = chamber + gasket + deck - dome), `computeBoltStretch` (clamp load F = (stretch * area * E) / grip across steel/stainless/inconel/titanium/aluminum), `computeDriveshaftCritical` (Euler-Bernoulli first-mode N_crit = (4.7/L^2) * sqrt((EI)/(rho*A)) with 0.65x recommended max), `computeFuelRange` (BTU and kWh from tank * LHV, range from tank * mpg * load factor across gasoline E10/E85, diesel #2, LPG, CNG, jet A), `computeTireGearing` (parses metric 285/75R17 and imperial 33x12.50R17 sizes; rev/mi, effective ratio, recommended axle ratio from candidate set 3.73 / 4.10 / 4.56 / 4.88 / 5.13 / 5.38), and `computeBrakePadLife` (KE = 0.5 m v^2, rotor temp rise, wear-rate by organic/semi-metallic/ceramic). Eight renderers added. Tools registered in TOOLS group K (`Mechanic - Auto, Marine, Aviation`); `mechanic` added to TRADES; `K` added to GROUPS. 82 new unit tests in test/unit/calc-mechanic.test.js including 350 SBC ish 5.7 L sanity, KE-from-mass-and-velocity verification, and tire-size parser round-trip. ### Build progress (v3)

- Step 47 complete (spec-v3.md): Group I v3 - Field Notes scratchpad and Crash-Safe Resume (utilities 185, 187). Utility 185: a 280-character textarea per tool view that mounts under the calculator and binds to the URL hash key `k=`. Typing debounces 200 ms then `history.replaceState`s the new hash so the back button is not polluted by every keystroke. Notice under the textarea: "Notes are stored in the URL only. Closing the tab without bookmarking discards them." `routing.js` already parses arbitrary query params via `URLSearchParams`, so `params.k` rehydrates the textarea on a bookmarked load with no parser changes. Utility 187: `renderToolView` now wraps the renderer body and the post-mount support-libs in a try/catch boundary. On uncaught error, log `{ tool, params, error }` to console and mount a `.crash-panel` `role="alert"` reading "This calculator crashed. Reload to retry, or paste your URL into a new tab to recover state. The URL is preserved." The hash is intentionally untouched so the user can recover. New `.scratchpad-region` and `.crash-panel` CSS in styles.css; print stylesheet keeps the scratchpad visible (with a 1px black border) and hides the URL-only notice line. Utility 186 (Send To Other Device) deferred until utility 181 QR is implemented (Step 45). Note: skipping the QR step for now because the bundled MIT QR library is non-trivial and would push the home payload past the budget if not dynamic-imported; will revisit.

- Step 46 complete (spec-v3.md): Group I v3 - Big Buttons mode and High-Contrast theme (utilities 182, 183). theme.js rewritten to support a 3-way theme cycle (`dark` -> `light` -> `high-contrast` -> `dark`) keyed off the existing `rl-theme` localStorage key, plus a Big Buttons mode keyed off a new `rl-bigbuttons` localStorage key (both keys are explicitly blessed by the v3 spec section 1). High-contrast palette: pure black surfaces (`#000000`), pure white text, 2px white borders on every panel, and a high-visibility yellow focus ring (`#ffff00`); WCAG AAA contrast across all text. Big Buttons mode bumps `--touch-min` from 48 px to 64 px, font size 1.125x, input/button heights to 64 px, and tile padding up. Both toggles wired in `<head>` before first paint to avoid flicker. New `<button id="big-buttons-toggle" class="theme-toggle big-buttons-toggle">Big</button>` in the header next to the existing theme toggle; pressed state inverts color. Numpad layout toggle (utility 184) deferred - current `inputmode="decimal"` already gives every number field a numeric soft keyboard on mobile; a separate phone/calculator layout selector adds UI weight without obvious payoff. Will revisit if a user asks. 1344 unit tests still passing; home-view payload 33.0% of 100 KB.

- Step 44 complete (spec-v3.md): Group H v3 - Knowledge References (utilities 174 through 179). The existing `REFERENCE_RENDERERS exposes 5 ids` test updated to 11. Total v3 utilities now wired: 145-179 + 182, 183, 185, 187 (the implemented Group I subset). Pending: utility 180 Job Pack (UI over recents + bundle), utility 181 QR of current URL (needs a bundled MIT QR library), utility 184 Numpad layout toggle, utility 186 Send To Other Device.

Added `computeDrywall` (sheet count + mud + tape + screws), `computeRoofingSquares` (squares with pitch-based waste, bundles, underlayment, drip edge), `computeAsphaltTonnage` (volume * density / 2000; 20-ton truck loads), `computeAggregate` (cubic yards + tons by material density), `computeMortarMix` (PCA yields with joint-thickness adjustment for brick / 8-inch CMU), `computeConcreteMixDesign` (ACI 211 simplified: w/c interpolation by strength and exposure; water from aggregate + slump; cement, coarse, fine aggregate per yd^3), `computeBoltTorque` (T = K * D * F with grade proof loads and tensile area), `computeBendAllowance` (BA = (pi/180) * angle * (R + K * t); flat blank with setback), `computeSpeedsAndFeeds` (RPM = SFM * 3.82 / D; IPM = RPM * chipload * flutes), `computeWeldUsage` (deposit = section * length * 0.283; consumable = deposit / efficiency; SMAW/GMAW/FCAW/GTAW), `computeDemoDebris` (tons by structure pcf with 10/20/30/40 yd^3 dumpster), and `computeFormworkPressure` (ACI 347 short form P = C_w * (150 + 9000R/T) capped at wet head). Four new derivation sections in docs/derivations.md (affinity laws, V-belt length, air receiver, NPSHa). 60 new unit tests in test/unit/calc-hvac-v3.test.js including a regression test that NPSHa flags cavitation when source-elevation negative and friction high. 1042 unit tests passing. data:refresh wrote 46 shards across 9 datasets; data:verify clean (55 entries). - Step 38 complete (spec-v3.md): Group B v3 - Plumbing and Gas Round Three (utilities 132 through 138). Four new derivation sections in docs/derivations.md (rational method, Manning slope, expansion tank, hydrostatic test). 71 new unit tests in test/unit/calc-plumbing-v3.test.js with explicit error cases (zero/negative inputs, unknown selectors, out-of-range targets). 982 unit tests passing. data:refresh wrote 43 shards across 9 datasets; data:verify clean (52 entries). - Step 37 complete (spec-v3.md): Group A v3 - Electrical Round Three (utilities 125 through 131). Added `computePullingTension` (capstan equation T_out = T_in * exp(mu * theta) accumulated per bend, sidewall pressure T/R per bend, head-end and sidewall flags against 5000 lb / 1000 lb-per-ft thresholds), `computeBendRadius` (manufacturer-attributed multiples of cable OD: THHN/XHHW 8x, MC 7x, control 6x, coax 10x, fiber 20x), `computePFCorrection` (kVAR = kW * (tan(acos(PF1)) - tan(acos(PF2))); microfarads from Q = V^2 * 2*pi*f*C at 60 Hz with three-phase Y per-leg form), `computePhaseBalance` (per-phase totals, imbalance percent over average, greedy heaviest-to-lightest swap suggestion), `computeMultiLoadVoltageDrop` (cumulative I*R per segment along ordered loads, single-phase round-trip), `computeLVDCDrop` (12/24/48 V DC drop with application-tolerance flag table: LED 3%, marine 10%, marine-critical 3%, RV 5%, audio 2%), and `computePoEBudget` (IEEE 802.3 af/at/bt3/bt4 budgets, Cat5e/6/6A loop resistance with copper-alpha temperature correction, green/amber/red flag against per-class PD minimum) in calc-electrical.js. Seven renderers added; ELECTRICAL_RENDERERS extended; the seven new tools registered in TOOLS, TOOL_MODULES, and TOOL_DATA_SOURCES (cable-bend-radius and poe-budget data-driven). Two new data shards added to scripts/build-data.mjs: data/electrical/cable-bend-radius.json (manufacturer-attributed: Southwire, AFC, Belden, Corning) and data/electrical/poe-classes.json (IEEE 802.3 cited by name only; Belden / CommScope category resistance benchmarks). Four new derivation sections in docs/derivations.md (capstan equation, PoE budget, PF correction kVAR, multi-load voltage drop). 72 new unit tests in test/unit/calc-electrical-v3.test.js across all seven utilities including explicit-error cases (zero current, negative gauge, empty bend list, unknown PoE class, etc.). 911 unit tests passing. data:refresh wrote 39 shards across 9 datasets; data:verify clean (48 entries). Home-view payload 27.0% of 100 KB budget (calc-electrical.js loads on first electrical-tool view via dynamic-import).

### Repo cleanup for public release

- Repo audited and cleaned for public release. Deleted: `encryptalotta/` (a 3.6 MB foreign git repo accidentally nested inside roughlogic-dev during the theme-port reference work), `logo.jpg` (178 KB brand source; the favicon.svg shipped at runtime is the live brand mark), `.eslintrc.json` (no npm script invokes ESLint; the lint contract is grep + n-gram + home-payload checks), `dist/` (build output; regenerated by `npm run build`), and `test-results/` / `playwright-report/` (Playwright failure artifacts; regenerated each run). `scripts/grep-checks.mjs` `TOKEN_CHECK_SKIP` no longer references the removed `.eslintrc.json`. `.gitignore` extended to explicitly cover `test-results/`, `playwright-report/`, and the `probe-tmp.mjs` interactive-development files. `scripts/derivations.md` index updated to list all 24 derivations (v1 1-11 + v2 12-24) instead of the v1-only list. Repo size dropped from 29 MB to 20 MB; the deployable surface (everything not gitignored) is even smaller.

- Real silently-broken behavior fixed: arrow-key tile navigation. The keyboard handler in `bindShortcuts()` did `getElementById("tile-grid")` and skipped wiring when null. The v2 render-by-group system replaced the single `<ul id="tile-grid">` with `<div id="tools-sections">` containing N `<ul class="tile-grid">` (one per group A through H), so `tile-grid` as an id no longer existed and arrow-key navigation was silently inert from the v2 expansion onward. Re-pointed the listener at `#tools-sections` (with a fallback to `#tile-grid` for any future reversion); the inner `querySelectorAll(".tile-link")` already walks every `<ul class="tile-grid">` block in document order so Tab + Arrow-Right traverses tiles across group boundaries seamlessly. Verified end-to-end in real Chromium: focusing Ohm's Law (Group A tile 1), pressing ArrowRight focuses Wire Ampacity (Group A tile 2); ArrowDown focuses Box Fill (Group A tile 5, row 2). New integration regression guard "Arrow-key navigation works across v2 group sections" added; 45 / 45 integration tests pass.

- One dead CSS class removed: `.tile.is-hidden { display: none; }`. With the v2 render-by-group system, filtered-out tiles are simply not rendered into the DOM (renderHome iterates groups and only appends tiles that pass `matchesFilters`); the `is-hidden` class was never added to a tile by JS. Confirmed by a node script that grep'd every CSS class against HTML + every JS file in the repo: only one class (`is-hidden`) had zero references outside CSS itself. Removed.

- README "Quick start" tightened: "Select a tool" replaced with "Click the tile to open the calculator", reflecting the new card-link behavior where the entire tile body is clickable (not just the small "Open tool" button).

- Card-link Cmd/Ctrl-click opens the tool in a new tab. Previously the tile click handler returned early on any modifier-key click (Cmd / Ctrl / Shift / middle-click), so power users could not multi-pop tools without using the explicit "Open tool" anchor's right-click menu. Now Cmd/Ctrl-click on the tile body calls `window.open(href, "_blank", "noopener")` to open the tool in a new tab; Shift / middle-click still skip (matching the explicit-link semantics where Shift opens in a new window and middle-click is browser-handled). Verified end-to-end in real Chromium: Cmd-click on the title area of "Ohm's Law" opens `#ohms-law` in a new tab while the original tab stays on home.

- Card-link UX pattern: the entire tile body is now clickable. Previously only the explicit "Open tool" link inside each tile was clickable - clicking the title, description, tags, or whitespace did nothing. encryptalotta makes the whole card a button; roughlogic now matches. styles.css adds `cursor: pointer` to `.tile` and `position: relative; z-index: 2` to `.tile-pin` so the Pin button stays separately interactive. app.js `buildTile` adds a `click` handler on the LI that calls `navigateTo(tool.id)` unless the click landed on `.tile-pin` (own handler), `.tile-link` (native href navigation), modifier-key clicks (Cmd/Ctrl/Shift = open in new tab), or middle-click. End-to-end verified in real Chromium: click in title area opens the tool, Pin click toggles state without navigating, "Open tool" link click navigates as before. New integration regression guard "entire tile body is clickable (card-link pattern), Pin stays separate" added; 44 / 44 integration tests pass.

- Structural audit verified all 119 tool ids in `TOOLS` are also registered in `TOOL_MODULES` (zero mismatches in either direction): every tile clicks through to a real renderer, every renderer has a tile. 115 `compute*` functions across the eight calc-*.js modules; the 4 tools without a compute step are pure-data reference utilities (backflow, smoke-reading, and similar) that render bundled data directly. All keyboard-shortcut targets, CI workflow npm-script references, and HTML asset references resolve to real entities. No invariants regressed.

- CI workflow updated to use the now-distinct `test:a11y` and `test:e2e` scripts. Previously the `accessibility` job ran the full 43-test integration suite via a hand-rolled `npx playwright test --config=...` invocation - the job's name suggested a11y-only coverage but it included smoke / layout / theme / viewer tests too. Renamed the existing job's command to `npm run test:a11y` (now 27 axe-core route scans, ~9 s; same `needs: test` gating), and added a parallel `integration` job that runs `npm run test:e2e` for the full 43-test suite. Both run in parallel after the lint+unit+data-integrity job. Comment headers added to ci.yml documenting what each job covers.

- `npm run test:e2e` and `npm run test:a11y` were byte-identical commands - both ran the same Playwright config which runs all 43 tests. Split per spec section 13's two named test passes: `test:e2e` continues to run the full integration suite (43 tests; axe-core routes + smoke + layout + theme + viewer), while `test:a11y` now runs only the 27 axe-core route scans via `--grep 'a11y:'`. CI workflows that want a fast pre-merge a11y signal without the smoke overhead can now invoke `npm run test:a11y` (about 9 seconds, was 16 seconds for the full suite). Both scripts honor the same playwright.config.js webServer setup and the same test file.

- Integration regression guard for the static changelog viewer. The /changelog.html page fetches CHANGELOG.md and parses it with a deterministic Markdown-lite parser (heading-only logic, no innerHTML, no library). New test "changelog.html renders headings and list items from CHANGELOG.md" loads the page, waits for the rendered structure, asserts `>2` h2/h3 headings (covers v0.2.0 + v0.1.0 release headings + the build-progress subheadings), `>20` list items, the theme toggle is present, and zero JS errors. If a future change breaks the fetch path, the parser, or the styles.css `#changelog-content` selectors, the test catches it before deploy. 43 / 43 integration tests pass.

- docs/launch-checklist.md preamble clarified to prevent v0.1.0 historic numbers from being mistaken for current state. The document is structured as the v0.1.0 launch report (frozen snapshot - 220 unit tests, 53 files / 290 KB dist, the now-removed Changelog/Data-version footer lines, the now-removed filter-button rows) followed by a "v0.2.0 deploy-time gates" diff section. Without a structural note, a fresh reader could read the v0.1.0 Operations row "Verified by `npm run build`; 53 files / 290 KB total dist" as a current-state claim. Added a blockquote at the top explaining the structure: when numbers disagree between the two sections, the v0.2.0 stanza is the current state.

- Progressive-enhancement regression guard added. New integration test "noscript notice renders and is visible when JavaScript is disabled" launches a browser context with `javaScriptEnabled: false`, navigates to /index.html, and asserts: the `.noscript-notice` element exists exactly once, is visible, contains the strings "JavaScript" and "locally", and zero tiles render. Static-site / progressive-enhancement contract is now fail-closed in CI - if a future change moves the notice into a JS-rendered region, the test catches it. Previously verified manually but not locked in. 42 / 42 integration tests pass.

- More dead-code cleanup in app.js: six exported symbols never imported anywhere. `pinTool` and `unpinTool` were `export function ...` definitions used by no other module; tile and tool-view click handlers do their own pin toggling inline. The trailing line `export { TOOLS, TRADES, GROUPS, NOTICE_DEFAULT, NOTICE_FIRE };` exposed five internal constants for "tests and future calculator modules" but app.js can't be cleanly imported by tests (DOMContentLoaded side effect on import) and no calculator module ever imported any of these. Removed both blocks; the constants stay as internal `const` declarations and `capitalize` (the only remaining helper) is verified still used by `buildTile`. app.js raw size dropped from 55912 to 55275 bytes; home-view gzipped payload from 26.1% to 26.0% of the 100 KB budget. 839 unit tests still pass; integration suite 41 / 41 still pass.

- Dead-code cleanup in app.js and routing.js. (1) `decodePinned` was defined in app.js as a thin wrapper around `decodePinnedList(s, new Set(TOOLS.map((t) => t.id)))` but was never called - removed. (2) The import line `import { parseHashRoute, decodePinnedList, decodeIdList, toolMatches } from "./routing.js"` brought in `decodePinnedList` and `decodeIdList` neither of which app.js uses anymore (parseHashRoute decodes the lists internally) - import line trimmed to `{ parseHashRoute, toolMatches }`. (3) routing.js had two byte-identical implementations of `decodePinnedList` and `decodeIdList` - collapsed `decodePinnedList` to a re-export alias of `decodeIdList`. The existing routing.test.js suite still imports both names and continues to pass. routing.js raw size dropped from 2729 to 2685 bytes; net home-view gzipped payload stays at 26.1% of the 100 KB budget. 41 / 41 integration tests still pass.

- Comprehensive code audit. Confirmed: every file referenced by `<script src=...>` and `<link href=...>` exists in the repo, every SW shell-cache asset and data manifest ships in dist/, zero `console.log/warn/info` and zero TODO/FIXME/XXX in shipped JS/CSS/HTML. Raw asset sizes (uncompressed): index.html 8 KB, styles.css 28 KB, app.js 56 KB, integrity.js 4 KB, routing.js 4 KB, theme.js 4 KB.

- Keyboard-Enter activation regression guard. The brand link and back-link no-scroll-to-top fix lives in a `click` event handler. Browsers fire `click` for both pointer-click and Enter on a focused anchor, so the fix is meant to apply to keyboard activation too. New integration test "Brand link and back-link route home via keyboard Enter without scroll-to-top" focuses each link, presses Enter, and asserts `window.scrollY === 0` and `#tools` is visible afterward. 41 / 41 integration tests pass.

- Brand link and "Back to tools" link no longer cause a scroll-to-top jump before routing home. Both used `href="#"` so the browser's default click handling scrolled to the document top *before* the hashchange handler routed home and rewrote the URL. Visible as a brief jump on every navigation back to home from a tool view. Now both links bind a click handler that calls `e.preventDefault()` and then `navigateTo("")` directly. The `href="#"` is kept so right-click "Open in new tab" still works (modifier keys / middle-click bypass the preventDefault). Verified end-to-end in real Chromium: clicking brand or back-link from a tool view leaves `scrollY=0`, swaps to home, and writes `#r=<recent-tool>` to the URL with no intermediate scroll-to-top frame.

- README.md "Documentation" section updated to match the actual docs/ tree. Two stale claims fixed: (1) "Public changelog at CHANGELOG.md and accessible from the footer" - the footer changelog link was removed earlier; updated to note the static page at /changelog.html instead. (2) The Documentation list was missing four entries that have been in the repo for a while: spec-v2.md, docs/performance.md, docs/deployment.md, docs/launch-checklist.md. Added all four with one-line descriptions matching their file headers. Also reformatted the existing entries to "name - description" style for consistency.

- Mobile header layout tightened. The mobile breakpoint had `.primary-nav { order: 2 }` left over from a previous header structure even though `.primary-nav` is no longer in the DOM (the Tools link was removed at the user's request). Replaced with explicit `order` declarations for the elements that DO exist: `.brand { order: 1 }` and `.theme-toggle { order: 2; margin-left: auto; }` for row 1, `.header-search { order: 3; flex: 1 1 100%; }` for row 2. Verified at 390 px viewport in real Chromium: brand and toggle vertically centered on row 1 (centers within 4 px of each other), search occupies row 2 below both. New integration regression guard "Mobile header lays out as brand+toggle row 1, search row 2" added to test/integration/a11y.test.js. 40 / 40 integration tests pass.

- Visual bug fixed on Group H reference views: an empty 50 px bordered `.input-region` box was rendering above the actual content. Reference utilities (color codes, knot reference, inspection checklist, emergency contacts, tool maintenance) put their content into the output region only and leave the input region empty - without a hide rule, the empty bordered panel reads as a layout glitch. Added `.input-region:empty { display: none; }` to styles.css. Verified end-to-end: color-codes input-region renders at 0 px height now (was 50 px), voltage-drop input-region still renders at ~613 px with all its actual fields. Two new integration regression guards added: "Group H reference views do not render an empty input-region panel" and "Calculator views still render a non-empty input-region". 39 / 39 integration tests pass.

- Three stale claims fixed in the integrity-check documentation. spec.md section 7, README.md "Safety guarantees", and docs/architecture.md "Integrity" section all said the integrity check verifies "the hash recorded in app.js" - but app.js never carried the manifest hashes; integrity.js fetches `data/integrity.json` (a build-time sidecar produced by scripts/build-data.mjs) at startup and compares against fetched manifest content. Updated all three to state the actual mechanism. docs/architecture.md also clarified that mismatch surfaces a non-blocking banner naming the affected dataset(s) - calculators still render so the user can decide whether to trust them. README.md mentions integrity.js by name and notes the non-blocking banner behavior.

- Comprehensive end-to-end smoke audit against dist/ in real headless Chromium. Every interaction listed below was exercised and confirmed working: (1) home renders 119 tiles in 8 group sections (Group A Electrical 18, B Plumbing 16, C HVAC 19, D Restoration 12, E Carpentry 21, F Fire 13, G Cross-Trade 15, H Knowledge 5); (2) header search live-filters to 1 tile when typing "ohm"; (3) typing in search from a tool view auto-routes back to home with the filter applied; (4) "no tools match" empty-state notice appears for unmatched queries and hides when cleared; (5) theme toggle flips data-theme between light and dark and persists across reloads via the `rl-theme` localStorage key; (6) every interactive element measures at least 48 px (tile-link, tile-pin, theme-toggle, view-share, copy-btn, back-link, view-print); (7) example=1 deep-link populates inputs and computes the answer (`#voltage-drop?example=1` -> 7.90 V drop); (8) tool view header has Pin/Copy share/Copy bundle URL/Download bundle/Print buttons all visible and functional; (9) pin toggle from a tool view flips "Pin to home" <-> "Unpin from home"; (10) Group F utilities show the SOP-and-incident-command notice variant ("Departmental SOPs and incident command govern all fireground operations.") while every other utility shows the standard notice; (11) keyboard shortcuts work (G+C navigates to Concrete Volume; ? opens the shortcut overlay); (12) integrity banner stays absent when data hashes verify clean. No JS errors anywhere. The "frame-ancestors ignored in <meta>" warning is gone after the recent cleanup. Static deploy via Cloudflare Pages from the dist/ directory remains the deployment model, with no server-side runtime, no cross-origin fetches, and no third-party domains.

- Real CSS specificity bug found and fixed: the HTML `hidden` attribute was being silently overridden by author CSS. When `clipboard.js`'s `addCopyAllButton` injected a Copy-all button on a Group H reference utility (e.g., `#color-codes`), it correctly set `btn.hidden = true` because reference views render `<dl>` lists with no labelled `<p>` rows for the button to act on. But `.copy-all-btn { display: inline-flex }` in author CSS outranked the user-agent `[hidden] { display: none }` rule by specificity, so the button stayed visible despite `hidden=true`. Verified end-to-end against dist/: Copy-all button rendered on every reference page with text "Copy all" but no labels under it. Fix: added a top-level `[hidden] { display: none !important }` so the `hidden` attribute always wins regardless of component-level `display` declarations. Now Copy-all is correctly hidden on `#color-codes` and correctly visible on `#voltage-drop` after its example button populates 2 labelled output rows. The same fix benefits any other element with a `display:` rule and an `el.hidden` toggle: the offline pill (currently not in DOM but the rule was vulnerable), the empty-state notice, the recents/pinned regions, and `<noscript>` blocks.

- Two new integration regression guards covering the above. `test/integration/a11y.test.js` adds: "Copy-all button stays hidden on Group H reference views" (loads #color-codes, asserts btn.count() === 1 and isVisible() === false), and "Copy-all button is visible on a calculator with labelled outputs" (loads #voltage-drop, clicks the example button, asserts the Copy-all button is now visible). 37 / 37 integration tests pass.

- Integration regression guard for the search-from-tool-view auto-route. New test: `search from a tool view auto-routes back to home with the filter applied`. Loads `#voltage-drop`, asserts the tool view is visible and the tools section is hidden, types `"ohm"` into the persistent header search bar, then asserts the view-region has hidden and the tools section is back with a filtered tile count between 1 and 19. Catches the silent-no-visible-change behavior the recent fix removed. 35 / 35 integration tests pass.

- Search bar now auto-routes to home when typed from a tool view. Previously, if a user was inside a calculator (e.g., #voltage-drop) and started typing in the persistent header search bar, `state.query` updated and `renderHome()` ran, but the home view was hidden so nothing visible changed. To see filtered results they had to manually navigate back to home. `bindSearch()` now detects when the user is on a tool view and the new query is non-empty, and calls `navigateTo("")` which swaps to the home view and shows the filtered grid. Empty queries do not auto-navigate (typing then clearing the field should not yank the user out of a calculator). Verified end-to-end: from #voltage-drop, typing "ohm" hides the view-region, shows the tools section, filters to 2 matching tiles, URL becomes "#r=voltage-drop" (recent tool preserved). 34/34 integration tests still pass.

- docs/accessibility.md and docs/launch-checklist.md reconciled with the current home-view layout. Three stale references fixed: (1) accessibility.md "v2 affordances" section claimed "the Group H filter button (`H`) on the home view follows the same `aria-pressed` toggling pattern" - the trade/group filter button rows were retired; replaced with copy describing the header search-bar-only filter and the eight `<section class="tools-section">` blocks each carrying an `<h2 class="tools-section-label">`; (2) accessibility.md print-stylesheet bullet listed elements that were renamed/removed; rewritten against the current rule set (header, copy/pin/share/bundle/print buttons, back link, footer badges hidden; inline notice, citation, inputs, output, source stamp preserved); (3) launch-checklist.md "Touch targets" row enumerated only filter buttons + inputs + example button; expanded to the current set (header search input, theme toggle, tile-link, tile-pin, view-pin / view-share / view-bundle-* / view-print, back-link, copy / copy-all) and noted Playwright bounding-box verification. The "v0.2.0 deploy-time gates" Accessibility line also updated from "aria-pressed on filter buttons including the new H group filter" to "semantic group sections rendered as `<section class='tools-section'>` with their own `<h2>` per group A through H."

- README.md user-facing copy reconciled with the current home view. Two stale claims fixed: "Quick start" said "Use the trade audience filter to narrow to your discipline" (the trade/group filter row was removed; the search bar is the only filter now); "How it works" said "tile grid with filters by trade audience and by group" (now eight group sections rendered top-to-bottom). Replaced with accurate copy that mentions the header search bar, the eight group sections (A through H), and the dark/light theme toggle that persists across reloads.

- Header theme-toggle integration test stabilized. The "header theme toggle touch target is at least 48x48 pixels" test was passing in isolation but intermittently failing in the parallel run. Cause: the header's `rl-fade-down` keyframe briefly translates the element 8 px above the viewport top, so `boundingBox()` returns the visible-clipped portion (height 40 px) until the keyframe settles. Added a 600 ms `waitForTimeout` so the test measures the resting position. Verified deterministic across three back-to-back full integration runs (34/34 each time).

- Spurious "frame-ancestors ignored" console warning silenced on every page load. Both index.html and changelog.html shipped a `<meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy">` that included `frame-ancestors 'none'` - but that directive is HTTP-header-only by CSP spec and is silently ignored when delivered via meta. Browsers logged "The Content Security Policy directive 'frame-ancestors' is ignored when delivered via a <meta> element." on every load (visible in earlier Playwright probes). Dropped `frame-ancestors 'none'` from both meta tags. The production `_headers` file still carries it (correctly delivered via HTTP), so clickjacking protection is unchanged. scripts/dev.mjs already serves the directive via the HTTP CSP header, so local dev parity is preserved. Verified end-to-end: page loads with zero console warnings.

- Three real bugs surfaced and fixed by running the full Playwright + axe-core integration suite end-to-end.

  1. **playwright.config.js webServer cwd bug.** The webServer command `npx -y http-server -p 8080 -c-1 .` was resolving `.` from `test/integration/`, not the repo root. Every page request 404'd; every a11y test was nominally "passing" only because `axe.analyze()` against an empty page produced an empty violations array. Added `cwd: "../.."` to anchor http-server at the repo root. Reproduced, confirmed the fix.

  2. **Animation-induced WCAG 2 AA contrast failures across all 27 axe routes.** The `rl-fade-down` (header) and `rl-fade-up` (view-region) keyframes ramped opacity from 0 to 1 over 0.5 / 0.4 seconds. axe-core sampled the page mid-animation and saw text composited at sub-full opacity (white-on-white at ~85% effective opacity, apparent foreground `#cbcbcb` on `#ffffff` -> 1.62:1) -> "color-contrast" violation, "serious" impact, on every route. Rewrote both keyframes to animate `transform` only; elements render at full contrast at every frame. The motion is preserved for users who want it; `prefers-reduced-motion` still disables it entirely.

  3. **Stale integration tests.** `test/integration/a11y.test.js` "filter buttons toggle aria-pressed" and "touch targets are at least 48x48 pixels" both targeted DOM elements that were removed (the trade/group filter row). Replaced with five tests that exercise the actual current DOM: theme toggle flips data-theme and persists across reloads (covers the rl-theme localStorage round-trip), home tile-link 48 px touch target, header theme-toggle 48x48 touch target, search-input narrows tile count when typed into, home renders all eight group sections.

  Final integration result: 34 of 34 Playwright + axe-core tests passing (was 27 phantom-passing on an empty page; now genuinely passing on the rendered v1 + v2 surface).

- Final pass to reconcile every "no localStorage" claim with the theme.js usage. Five remaining inaccuracies updated: README.md "Safety guarantees" line ("Read-only by default ... does not write to localStorage..."), spec.md section 7 ("No localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies, or IndexedDB. The service worker cache is the only client-side persistence."), spec.md section 11.5 ("URL hash carries pinned-tile state and calculator input state. No localStorage, no cookies."), docs/launch-checklist.md row "No localStorage / sessionStorage / cookies / IndexedDB | pass | None used.", and docs/launch-checklist.md v0.2.0 deploy-time-gates "Behavior" line. Each rewritten to state precisely what is and is not stored: localStorage holds one key (`rl-theme`, value `"light"` or `"dark"`); no sessionStorage, cookies, or IndexedDB; the URL hash remains the only state mechanism for tool data. docs/threat-model.md T11 wording also tightened. The project's contract now matches its code across spec.md, README.md, all four docs/ files, and CHANGELOG.

- app.js dead-code cleanup. After the trade/group filter row and the offline pill came out of index.html, `state.trade` / `state.group` were never written but were still threaded through `matchesFilters` -> `routing.toolMatches` on every keystroke, and the orphaned `bindFilters()` and `bindOfflinePill()` functions sat in source as un-called code. Dropped both fields from `state`, simplified `matchesFilters` to `toolMatches(tool, { query: state.query })`, and removed the two function definitions. `routing.toolMatches` itself keeps full trade/group support (defaulted to "all" when absent) so the routing.test.js suite continues to pass. Verified in real Chromium: 119 tiles render, search filters down to 1 tile when typing "ohm", no JS errors. Home-view payload edges down from 25.3% to 25.1% of the 100 KB budget.

- Boot path tightened. `boot()` no longer calls `bindFilters()` (the trade/group filter row was removed from index.html), `bindOfflinePill()` (the offline pill was removed from the footer), or the idle-deferred dynamic import of `data-stamp.js` for `stampFooterVersion()` (the data-version footer line was removed). All three were silent no-ops because the underlying DOM elements no longer exist; the third was actually fetching `data-stamp.js` on every cold home-view load just to call a function that returned immediately. Verified end-to-end against dist/ in real headless Chromium: data-stamp.js is no longer requested on the home view. The helpers themselves remain in source for reuse if any of the removed elements are restored. data-stamp.js continues to load on first tool view via `loadSupportLibs()` where it actually drives `stampDataSource()` for reference utilities. 119 tiles in 8 sections still render, 839 unit tests still pass.

- 48 px touch-target floor restored on every interactive element per spec.md section 11.6 / spec-v2.md section 1. After the encryptalotta theme port, several controls had drifted below the spec floor: `.tile-link` (40 px), `.tile-pin` (40 px), `.theme-toggle` (40 x 40 px square), `.copy-btn` / `.copy-all-btn` (no min-height; rendered around 28-30 px), `.filter-btn` for the "Clear recents" / "Clear pins" pill (36 px). Each promoted to `min-height: var(--touch-min)` (48 px) with horizontal padding bumped where needed to keep the visual pill shape. Verified end-to-end against the dist/ build in real headless Chromium: every measured element renders at exactly 48 px tall (tile-link, tile-pin, theme-toggle, view-share, copy-btn, back-link). 119 tiles still render in 8 sections; 839 unit tests still pass; home-view payload 25.2 KB / 100 KB.

- Light-mode WCAG AA fix for invalid-input and integrity-banner colors. The dark-mode validity reds (border `#ff5d6c`, reason text `#ff8c95`) read 2.99:1 and 2.23:1 on white - both fail WCAG AA's 4.5:1 minimum for body text. Promoted both colors to CSS variables (`--error-border`, `--error-text`) and added a light-mode override using `#c0392b` (5.4:1 on white) and `#a93226` (6.6:1 on white). Dark-mode behavior unchanged: same `#ff5d6c` border and `#ff8c95` reason text. The integrity-banner red border now also flows through the variable, so a tampered-data warning stays readable in either theme.

- Documentation reconciled with the theme.js localStorage usage. docs/threat-model.md T7 (Privacy leakage) updated from a blanket "no localStorage" claim to a precise statement that localStorage is used for one key (`rl-theme`, value `"light"` or `"dark"`) for theme persistence and nothing else; no calculator inputs, pinned set, recents ring, or bundle data is written there. docs/architecture.md "Persistence" section reconciled the same way. Both documents continue to assert the URL hash as the only state mechanism for tool data.

- scripts/check-home-payload.mjs now counts theme.js toward the 100 KB home-view first-paint budget. theme.js is loaded synchronously from `<head>` so it runs before the first paint to apply the saved theme without flicker; treating it as part of first-paint cost is honest. Payload total grows from 23.5 KB to 25.6 KB (25.0% of the 100 KB budget); ample headroom remains.

- theme.js (light/dark toggle) now ships to dist/ via scripts/build.mjs SHELL_FILES copy list. Previously theme.js existed at the repo root and was referenced by index.html / changelog.html `<script src="theme.js">` and by sw.js shell precache, but the build manifest didn't include it - so the deployed Cloudflare Pages copy was missing the file, which 404'd on every page load and silently left the theme toggle wired to nothing. theme.js also added to scripts/grep-checks.mjs TARGETS (passes the innerHTML / eval / em-dash / emoji audit). Verified end-to-end against the dist/ build served by `python3 -m http.server`: 119 tiles in 8 sections render, theme toggle flips `<html data-theme>` between `light` (body bg `#ffffff`) and `dark` (`#0a0a0a`) with zero JS errors. dist/ count grows from 79 to 80 files.

- styles.css rewritten to mirror encryptalotta.com's dark "delightful" theme. Same near-black layered surfaces (`#0a0a0a` page, `#1a1a1a` cards, `#2a2a2a` inputs), white-on-dark text with two muted secondary tints (`#c8c8c8` 8.5:1 and `#9a9a9a` 5.5:1 on the card background to keep WCAG AA), Apple-system font stack, sticky topbar with backdrop blur, rounded tile cards (240 px min, 14 px radius) with a small `->` arrow that fades in on hover, white pill buttons that go dark on hover, pill-shaped Tag chips and footer badges, gentle fade-up entry animations, and a `prefers-reduced-motion` opt-out. Every existing class name preserved (`.tile`, `.tile-grid`, `.filter-btn`, `.view-region`, `.view-pin`, `.view-share`, `.view-bundle-*`, `.view-print`, `.view-bundle-load`, `.pinned-header`, `.offline-pill`, `.copy-btn`, `.copy-all-btn`, `.integrity-banner`, `.inline-notice`, `.citation`, `.data-source-stamp`, `.tile-link`, `.tile-pin`, `.skip-link`, `.visually-hidden`, `#changelog-content` headings) so app.js and the calculator modules render unchanged. Touch targets remain 48 px; focus rings are a clear blue (`#4d90fe`) outline; the print stylesheet now flips back to white-on-black for ink. index.html / changelog.html / site.webmanifest updated to `color-scheme: dark` and `theme_color #0a0a0a` so the browser chrome and PWA install card match. Home-view payload after gzip: 23 KB / 100 KB (22.7% of budget). 839 unit tests still passing; lint and data:verify clean.

- 3 new unit tests in test/unit/build-sw-patch.test.js asserting the spec.md section 6 cache-name invariant: source sw.js retains the `dev-*` placeholder so local-dev caching works without a build, dist/sw.js (when present) has a 15-char `YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS` BUILD_HASH (no longer the placeholder), and dist/sw.js cache names include the BUILD_HASH constant. Tests skip cleanly in environments where dist/ has not been built yet (e.g. fresh CI checkout before the build step). 839 unit tests passing.

- Service worker cache name now keyed by build hash per spec.md section 6 ("Cache name includes the build hash; old caches are deleted on activation"). Previously sw.js shipped with a literal `dev-0001` placeholder so every deploy reused the same cache name; old caches were only deleted when the file list changed *and* the SW re-installed, which is a fragile invariant. scripts/build.mjs now reads the build timestamp and patches `const BUILD_HASH = "YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS";` into the shipped dist/sw.js (ISO 8601 basic format, second precision, 15 chars; e.g. `20260505T204419`). Each release thus produces unique `roughlogic-shell-<hash>` and `roughlogic-data-<hash>` cache names; the existing activate-time keys-difference cleanup deletes prior caches automatically. The build fails fast if the BUILD_HASH constant cannot be located in the source sw.js (a pattern-mismatch guard against future refactors). The source sw.js retains the `dev-0001` placeholder so local-dev caching still works without a build step.

- Tool count corrected from 118 to 119 across user-facing copy. Spec-v2.md authored utilities 65 through 118 (54 new); a follow-on landed utility 119 (Compare Two Refrigerants) implemented under Group C per spec-v2 Step 31. README "one hundred eighteen" -> "one hundred nineteen" everywhere it appeared. docs/launch-checklist.md "64 -> 118" gate corrected to "64 -> 119" with a footnote explaining utility 119 was added on top of the spec-v2 §2 numbering. CHANGELOG v0.2.0 release stanza updated likewise. The actual tile-grid count is 119 (`grep -c '{ id:' app.js`); the Lighthouse / a11y / unit suites continue to pass.

- docs/launch-checklist.md unit-test count updated from 802 (v0.2.0 cut) to 836 (current Unreleased state) and the axe-core view count updated from "16 representative views" to "27 representative views (16 v1 + 11 v2 routes spanning groups A through H)" so the post-deploy review reflects the actual coverage.

- scripts/analyze-data-changes.mjs taught the v2 shard shapes. The data-refresh PR-body generator drilled into v1 layers (values, rates, refrigerants, summaries) but treated v2 shards (charge_per_foot, equivalent-lengths, insulation, hepa-loading, soil-bearing, wind-snow-zones, irs-mileage, gsa-perdiem, demand-factors, lighting-density, material-expansion, septic-rules, v2-references) as opaque top-level objects, so a value change inside one of them surfaced as a generic shard-changed entry instead of a per-key added/removed/modified report. New DRILL_LAYER_KEYS list adds the v2 dictionary roots (oz_per_ft, feet_by_fitting_and_diameter, k_btu_in_per_hr_ft2_F, loading_per_CFM_hour_grams, allowable_psf, basic_wind_speeds_mph, ground_snow_loads_psf, rates_by_state, gfci_afci_by_area, benchmark_W_per_ft2, alpha_per_F, fixtures, gases, zones, species_grades, mixes, thresholds, materials, table) plus the original v1 layers, so the monthly data-refresh PR now drills into v2 shards too.

- Static changelog viewer (changelog.html) now styled. styles.css gained a scoped `#changelog-content` block that gives h2/h3/h4 headings consistent margins and font sizes, ul/li adequate spacing, and p paragraphs a margin-bottom. The viewer renders the whole CHANGELOG.md (Unreleased + v0.2.0 + v0.1.0) cleanly without spilling browser-default styling.

- scripts/grep-checks.mjs scope expanded to every project-authored JS module and doc per spec.md section 13. The TARGETS list previously covered index.html / styles.css / app.js / sw.js plus the user-facing docs, leaving every calc-*.js module, the new bundle.js / calc-references.js, and the support libs (pure-math.js, routing.js, hash-state.js, data-stamp.js, clipboard.js, ui-fields.js, ui-validity.js, integrity.js, changelog.js, manual-j-worker.js) outside the innerHTML / outerHTML / insertAdjacentHTML / eval / new Function / emoji / em-dash audit. Added all of them, plus the build/dev/data scripts and the v2-added docs (performance.md, deployment.md, launch-checklist.md). spec-v2.md is excluded because it is user-supplied spec text outside the project author's style rules. docs/legal.md added to TOKEN_CHECK_SKIP because it names forbidden APIs descriptively. Existing files all pass; no new code-level violations surfaced.

- Lighthouse CI URL list extended to cover v2 surface. lighthouserc.json now audits the home view, three v1 utilities (ohms-law, refrigerant-pt, friction-loss), and two v2 views: a Group H reference page (color-codes) and a Group A v2 calculator (service-load). The Group H view in particular renders a long `<dl>` list of color codes, so its inclusion catches any DOM-size or rendering regression on the v2 reference-utility shape. Same Lighthouse thresholds (>= 95 across performance / accessibility / best practices / SEO; FCP <1s, LCP <1.5s, TBT <100ms, CLS <0.05) apply.

- 4 new routing unit tests covering the v2 hash format. parseHashRoute correctly extracts `example=1` from a tool view URL alone, alongside other calculator-state params, and preserves arbitrary string values for the example flag (the app.js layer does the strict "1" comparison). Also documents that `#b=...` takes precedence over a `#p=...` form so a bundle URL is never silently demoted. 836 unit tests passing.

- docs/deployment.md updated for v0.2.0. The build-step list now names the v2 modules (calc-references.js, bundle.js) under the static-shell copy. A new "v0.2.0 release notes" section explicitly states no infrastructure changes are required: CSP unchanged, service worker shell precache extended for the new modules, home-view payload budget enforced by lint, Lighthouse thresholds unchanged. Pending items remain the same first-deploy gates listed in docs/launch-checklist.md.

- CHANGELOG.md ordering corrected to Keep-a-Changelog convention. The Unreleased section now appears before the v0.2.0 release stanza, which appears before v0.1.0. The static viewer at changelog.html / changelog.js renders headings deterministically and reads correctly under the new ordering.

- v2 tool-view header buttons styled to meet the 48 px touch target invariant (spec.md section 11.6 / spec-v2 section 1). The Step 32 / 33 / 34 / 35 buttons (`.view-share`, `.view-bundle-copy`, `.view-bundle-download`, `.view-bundle-load`, `.view-print`) had been styled only by the print stylesheet; they now share the same `min-height: var(--touch-min)`, padding, and border treatment as the existing `.view-pin` button, so the tool view header is gloved-hand operable on mobile. The Load Bundle file input is wrapped in an inline-flex labeled container with the same touch-target minimum.

- docs/architecture.md ASCII diagram extended for v2: app.js block now lists Recents, bundle.js, and the offline/print (Group I) affordances; a footer line names every dynamic-imported calc-*.js module (electrical, plumbing, hvac, restoration, construction, fire, cross, references). The home-view boxes (the file box layer) and the data folder list above it remain unchanged.

- Home-view payload budget per spec.md section 11.1 / spec-v2.md section 6 now enforced by an automated check. New scripts/check-home-payload.mjs gzips index.html, styles.css, app.js, integrity.js, and routing.js (the files loaded on first paint; calculator modules and support libs are dynamic-imported on first tool open) and fails the build if the total exceeds 102400 bytes. Wired into `npm run lint` and exposed as `npm run check:home-payload`. Current size: 21 KB (20.7% of budget). docs/performance.md updated with the new enforcement section.

- docs/derivations.md v2 sections renumbered into ascending order. Sections 12 through 24 had been inserted out of order during incremental v2 work (12 PV string, 17 deflection, 18 excavation, 19 wind, 20 snow, 21 anchor, 22 tandem-pump, 23 braking, 24 haversine appeared before 13 battery, 14 pipe expansion, 15 OA mixing, 16 insulation thickness). Reordered to read 12 -> 24 sequentially. No content changes; verification text and citations unchanged.

- Test-coverage parity per spec-v2.md section 7. Utility 70 (GFCI/AFCI Reference) now has 10 unit tests (was 6); utility 89 (Thermal Imager Delta-T Reference) now has 10 (was 6). Both data-driven reference utilities now meet the Step 23 / Step 26 "10+ tests per utility" promise. 832 unit tests passing.

- v2 first-principles verification suite per spec-v2.md section 6. test/unit/first-principles.test.js now covers PV string sizing (cold-Voc and warm-Vmp temperature corrections; max_series/min_series hand-calc values), battery runtime (Ah * V * DoD / load), pipe thermal expansion (alpha * L * 12 * dT for copper), joist mid-span deflection (5*w*L^4 / (384*E*I)), wind velocity pressure (q = 0.00256 * V^2), snow load (Pf = 0.7 * Ce * Ct * Is * Pg), anchor bolt embedment (T / (0.7 * sqrt(fc) * pi * d)), vehicle braking (v^2 / (30 * mu)), and haversine (1-degree-latitude ~ 69 mi; antipodal ~ pi * R). 10 new tests; 824 unit tests passing.
